indicators.desc_en: Measures the change in prices paid by urban consumers for a basket of goods and services. The year-over-year change is the most-watched inflation metric in the US. Above 2% signals elevated inflation; the Fed targets 2% over time.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量城市消費者購買商品和服務的價格變化。年增率是美國最受關注的通膨指標。超過 2% 代表通膨偏高，聯準會的長期通膨目標為 2%。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 CPI（不含食品與能源）
indicators.desc_en: CPI excluding volatile food and energy prices. Core CPI is the Fed's preferred short-term inflation gauge because it filters out supply-shock noise. Persistently high core CPI is a strong signal for rate hikes.
indicators.desc_zh: 剔除波動較大的食品和能源的 CPI。核心 CPI 是聯準會偏好的短期通膨指標，因為它過濾了供給衝擊的噪音。核心 CPI 持續偏高是升息的強烈訊號。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 PCE 物價指數
indicators.desc_en: The Fed's officially preferred inflation measure. PCE covers a broader range of spending than CPI and adjusts for substitution effects. The FOMC explicitly targets 2% Core PCE as its long-run goal.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會官方偏好的通膨衡量指標。PCE 涵蓋比 CPI 更廣泛的消費支出，且會調整替代效應。聯邦公開市場委員會明確以 2% 核心 PCE 為長期目標。

indicators.desc_en: Measures average change in prices received by domestic producers. PPI is a leading indicator for CPI—rising producer costs often get passed on to consumers. A surge in PPI often precedes consumer inflation by 1-3 months.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量國內生產者收到的價格平均變化。PPI 是 CPI 的領先指標，生產成本上升往往會轉嫁給消費者，PPI 飆升通常比消費通膨早 1-3 個月出現。

indicators.name_zh: 密大消費者通膨預期（一年期）
indicators.desc_en: University of Michigan survey measuring consumer expectations for inflation over the next year. Anchored inflation expectations are critical for central bank credibility. A jump above 4% raises concern about a wage-price spiral.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學調查消費者對未來一年通膨的預期。通膨預期是否穩定對央行公信力至關重要，若飆破 4% 將引發工資-物價螺旋上升的疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Number of jobs added to the economy (ex-farm & household). The most market-moving monthly release. Above +150K is generally healthy; below +100K signals slowdown. The Fed watches NFP closely alongside inflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國經濟新增的非農就業人數，是每月最撼動市場的數據。超過 +15 萬通常為健康；低於 +10 萬則暗示放緩，聯準會高度關注此數據。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking work. At 3.5-4%, the labor market is considered near-full employment. The Fed's dual mandate includes maximizing employment.
indicators.desc_zh: 正在積極求職的勞動力佔比。3.5-4% 附近被認為接近充分就業。聯準會的雙重任務包含最大化就業。

indicators.name_zh: 初次申請失業救濟金人數
indicators.desc_en: Weekly count of newly unemployed individuals filing for benefits. The most timely labor market indicator. A 4-week moving average above 300K often signals labor market deterioration. Markets are sensitive to sharp week-to-week moves.
indicators.desc_zh: 每週新申請失業救濟金的人數，是最即時的勞動市場指標。四週移動平均超過 30 萬通常代表勞動市場惡化，市場對每週大幅變動極為敏感。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of working-age population that is employed or actively job-seeking. Low participation can mask real unemployment. Post-COVID, the US LFPR has recovered but remains below pre-pandemic highs due to aging demographics.
indicators.desc_zh: 工作年齡人口中就業或積極求職的比例。低參與率可能掩蓋真實失業情況。疫情後美國勞動參與率已回升，但因人口老化，仍低於疫前高點。

indicators.desc_en: Broadest measure of labor underutilization, including part-time workers who want full-time jobs and marginally attached workers. U-6 typically runs 3-5 percentage points above U-3 (headline unemployment). A narrowing U3/U6 gap indicates a tightening labor market.
indicators.desc_zh: 最廣義的勞動力未充分利用指標，包含希望全職工作卻只能兼職者及邊緣勞工。U-6 通常比 U-3（標準失業率）高 3-5 個百分點，兩者差距收窄代表勞動市場緊俏。

indicators.desc_en: Inflation-adjusted total output of the US economy. Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth constitutes a technical recession. Healthy growth is generally 2-3% annualized. This is the definitive measure of economic health.
indicators.desc_zh: 經通膨調整的美國經濟總產出。連續兩季負成長即為技術性衰退。健康的成長率約為年化 2-3%，是最終極的經濟健康指標。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly measure of consumer spending at retail stores, excluding volatile auto sales. Consumer spending drives ~70% of US GDP, so strong retail sales signal economic resilience. A sharp miss can trigger equity market selloffs.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月零售商場消費支出，剔除波動較大的汽車銷售。消費支出佔美國 GDP 約 70%，強勁的零售銷售代表經濟韌性，大幅低於預期可能引發股市賣壓。

indicators.desc_en: Measures output of US factories, mines, and utilities. Industrial production accounts for about 15% of GDP but is more volatile and leads economic turning points. A sustained decline often precedes recession.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量美國工廠、礦業和公用事業的產出。工業生產佔 GDP 約 15%，波動較大但能領先經濟轉折點，持續下滑往往是衰退先兆。

indicators.desc_en: Total spending by US households on goods and services. PCE is broader than retail sales and is the primary component of GDP. A deceleration in PCE growth warns of economic softening even when headline unemployment stays low.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國家庭在商品和服務上的總支出，比零售銷售更廣泛，是 GDP 的主要組成部分。PCE 成長放緩是經濟趨軟的警示，即使失業率仍低。

indicators.desc_en: The interest rate at which banks lend reserve balances to each other overnight—the primary policy tool of the Federal Reserve. Rate hikes cool inflation but risk slowing growth; cuts stimulate the economy. Every basis point move ripples across all asset classes.
indicators.desc_zh: 銀行間隔夜拆借準備金的利率，是聯準會的主要政策工具。升息可抑制通膨但有壓縮成長的風險；降息可刺激經濟。每一個基點的變動都會波及所有資產類別。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new residential construction projects begun per month. Housing is a leading indicator—builders break ground based on demand expectations 6-18 months out. A drop in starts signals a cooling market well before prices adjust.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月開始動工的新住宅建設數量。房屋建設是領先指標，建商依據 6-18 個月後的需求預期動工。開工數下降是房市降溫的早期訊號，早於房價反應。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new construction permits issued monthly. Permits are issued before construction starts, making them an even more leading indicator than housing starts—often 1-2 months ahead. A permit trend closely tracks future housing supply.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月核發的新建築許可數量。許可在施工前核發，比新屋開工數更具領先性，通常早 1-2 個月反映趨勢，許可趨勢緊密追蹤未來住宅供給。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly count of completed sales of previously constructed homes. Existing home sales account for ~85% of total home sales and reflect real-time demand. A decline signals affordability stress, usually from high mortgage rates or prices.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月已建成住宅的成交量，佔全美房屋銷售約 85%，反映即時需求。銷量下滑代表購房負擔壓力增大，通常源於高房貸利率或高房價。

indicators.desc_en: Average interest rate for a 30-year fixed mortgage in the US. Mortgage rates typically move with the 10-year Treasury yield plus a spread. Above 7% significantly pressures housing affordability and can freeze transaction volume.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國 30 年期固定房貸平均利率。房貸利率通常隨 10 年期美債殖利率加上利差波動，超過 7% 會顯著壓縮購房可負擔性並可能凍結成交量。

indicators.desc_en: The gold standard for tracking US home price changes across 20 major metropolitan areas using a repeat-sales methodology. Published with a 2-month lag. YoY changes above 10% signal speculative excess; consecutive monthly declines signal a correction.
indicators.desc_zh: 追蹤 20 個主要大都會區房價變化的黃金標準指標，採用重複銷售方法論。發布有約 2 個月滯後。年增率超過 10% 代表投機過熱，連續月跌代表房市修正。

indicators.desc_en: Total US manufacturing sector employment (BLS payrolls). A leading indicator for the goods-producing economy. Sustained YoY declines have historically preceded broader recessions.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國製造業總就業人數（勞工部薪資調查）。商品生產經濟的領先指標，年增率持續為負通常領先整體衰退。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly value of new orders placed for manufactured goods expected to last 3+ years (aircraft, machinery, vehicles). Highly volatile due to aircraft orders. The ex-defense, ex-aircraft 'core capital goods' measure is preferred for clean business investment trends.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月製造業新接的耐久財訂單（使用 3 年以上如飛機、機械、車輛）。因飛機訂單而波動極大。剔除國防和飛機的「核心資本財」指標更能反映乾淨的商業投資趨勢。

indicators.desc_en: Index measuring the real output of the manufacturing sector (subset of total industrial production). Captures factory floor activity in real time. A sustained decline in manufacturing production often precedes broader economic slowdowns by 2-3 quarters.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量製造業實際產出的指數（工業生產總指數的子集）。即時捕捉工廠生產動態。製造業生產持續下滑，往往比整體經濟放緩早 2-3 季出現。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly University of Michigan survey of consumer confidence about current and future economic conditions. Acts as both a coincident and leading indicator—a sharp drop in sentiment can become self-fulfilling as consumers pull back on spending.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學每月調查消費者對當前和未來經濟狀況的信心，兼具同步和領先指標功能。信心大幅下滑可能自我實現，因為消費者會縮減支出。

indicators.desc_en: The spread between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. An inverted curve (negative spread) has preceded every US recession since 1955 by 6-18 months. Re-steepening from inversion is often the most reliable recession imminence signal.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期與2年期美債殖利率差。曲線倒掛（負值）自1955年以來每次都領先美國經濟衰退6-18個月。從倒掛回正陡峭化，是衰退迫近的最可靠訊號之一。

indicators.desc_en: The 10-year minus 3-month Treasury spread—the NY Fed's preferred recession indicator. Inversion has accurately predicted 8 of the last 8 US recessions. Watched more closely by economists than 10Y-2Y.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期減3個月期美債利差，為紐約聯儲偏好的衰退指標。倒掛準確預測過去8次美國衰退中的全部8次，經濟學家比10年-2年利差更看重此指標。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note. Drives mortgage rates, corporate borrowing costs, and is the global risk-free benchmark. Rising 10Y yields pressure equity valuations, especially high-duration tech stocks.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期美國國債的基準殖利率，是房貸、企業借貸成本的指標，也是全球無風險利率基準。10年殖利率上升會壓縮股市估值，尤其衝擊長存續期的科技股。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on 2-year Treasury notes, closely tracking Fed policy expectations. The 2-year reflects what markets expect the Fed Funds rate to average over the next two years. Sharp moves often telegraph FOMC pivots before they happen.
indicators.desc_zh: 2年期美債殖利率，緊密追蹤聯準會政策預期。反映市場對未來兩年聯邦基金利率平均水準的預期，大幅波動常領先 FOMC 政策轉向。

indicators.desc_en: CBOE Volatility Index, measuring implied volatility on S&P 500 options. Below 15 = complacency; 20-30 = caution; above 40 = panic. VIX spikes correlate with sharp equity selloffs and flight-to-safety flows.
indicators.desc_zh: 芝加哥選擇權交易所波動率指數，衡量標普500選擇權的隱含波動率。低於15代表市場自滿，20-30為警戒，高於40為恐慌。VIX飆升常伴隨股市大跌與避險資金流入。

indicators.desc_en: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread—the premium investors demand to hold junk bonds vs. Treasuries. Below 400bps = risk-on; above 600bps = stress; above 1000bps = recession-grade panic. Leading indicator of credit cycle turns.
indicators.desc_zh: ICE BofA 美國高收益債券指數選擇權調整利差，投資人持有垃圾債相對美債所要求的風險溢酬。低於 400bps 為風險偏好，高於 600bps 為壓力，超過 1000bps 為衰退級恐慌。是信用循環轉折的領先指標。

indicators.desc_en: Fed's broad trade-weighted dollar index against major trading partners' currencies. A strong dollar pressures multinational earnings, commodity prices (negatively), and emerging market debt. A weakening dollar typically boosts US equities and EM assets.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會編製的貿易加權廣義美元指數，對美國主要貿易夥伴貨幣加權。強勢美元壓縮跨國企業獲利、商品價格與新興市場債務；弱勢美元通常利多美股與新興市場資產。

indicators.desc_en: West Texas Intermediate crude oil spot price per barrel. Oil prices feed into inflation, transportation costs, and consumer discretionary spending. Sustained moves above $90 typically pressure CPI; below $60 raises concerns about global demand.
indicators.desc_zh: 西德州中級原油現貨價格（每桶美元）。油價影響通膨、運輸成本與消費者可支配支出。持續高於 90 美元常推高 CPI；低於 60 美元則引發全球需求疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Broad measure of US money supply including currency, deposits, and money market funds. M2 YoY growth correlates with inflation 12-18 months ahead. Post-2022 contraction was the first since the Great Depression, signaling tight liquidity.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國廣義貨幣供給，含現金、存款與貨幣市場基金。M2 年增率領先通膨 12-18 個月。2022 年後的萎縮是自大蕭條以來首見，反映流動性緊縮。

indicators.desc_en: Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey from BLS—total unfilled job postings. The job openings to unemployed ratio (currently watched closely by Powell) signals labor market tightness. A drop below 1.0 typically precedes wage disinflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部 JOLTS 調查的未填補職位總數。職位空缺對失業人數比率（鮑爾密切關注的指標）反映勞動市場緊俏程度，低於 1.0 通常領先工資通膨降溫。

indicators.desc_en: Average hourly earnings of all private-sector employees. YoY growth above 4% is consistent with sticky 2%+ services inflation. Fed watches wage growth as the lynchpin of services inflation dynamics.
indicators.desc_zh: 民間部門全體勞工平均時薪。年增率高於 4% 與黏性 2%+ 服務業通膨一致。聯準會將工資成長視為服務業通膨動態的關鍵指標。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of industrial capacity actually in use. Above 80% indicates tight capacity (inflationary pressure); below 75% signals slack (deflationary). Recession averages historically run 73-75%.
indicators.desc_zh: 工業產能實際使用比例。高於 80% 代表產能吃緊（通膨壓力），低於 75% 代表閒置（通縮壓力）。歷史衰退期平均落在 73-75%。

indicators.name_zh: 費城聯儲領先經濟指數
indicators.desc_en: Philadelphia Fed's State Leading Index based on housing permits, jobless claims, manufacturing hours, and yield spread. Six consecutive negative months has historically signaled recession with 80%+ accuracy.
indicators.desc_zh: 費城聯儲編製，綜合建築許可、初領失業金、製造業工時與利差等領先指標。連續六個月為負歷史上以 80%+ 準確率預測衰退。

indicators.desc_en: Difference between US exports and imports of goods/services. Persistent deficits (currently ~$70B/month) pressure the dollar long-term. Sudden widening can signal domestic demand strength or import surges (often China-related).
indicators.desc_zh: 美國商品與服務出口減進口的差額。持續赤字（目前約每月 700 億美元）長期施壓美元。突然擴大可能反映內需強勁或進口激增（常與中國貿易相關）。

indicators.desc_en: BLS import price index covering all imported goods. A leading indicator for goods CPI—rising import prices typically pass through to consumer inflation within 1-3 quarters. Closely watched during trade-tension episodes.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部編製的全進口商品價格指數，是商品 CPI 的領先指標。進口物價上升通常 1-3 季後轉嫁至消費通膨，在貿易摩擦時期備受關注。

commentary.zh: 2026年4月消費者物價指數升至332.407，月增0.6%，顯示通膨壓力持續。自2025年4月以來，指數累計上漲3.8%，近三個月加速上升，特別是3月至4月跳增0.64%。若此趨勢延續，年增率將明顯超過聯準會2%目標。這表明物價上漲動能未減弱，可能促使聯準會維持較長時間的較高利率政策，以防止通膨預期錨定失敗。市場應關注下月數據是否確認升息週期延長的信號。
commentary.en: April's CPI reading of 332.407 marks a 0.6% monthly gain, signaling persistent inflationary pressures. The index has climbed 3.8% since April 2025, with acceleration evident in recent months—particularly March to April's 0.64% jump. This trajectory suggests year-over-year inflation likely remains substantially above the Fed's 2% target. Continued momentum could prompt the Fed to maintain elevated rates longer than markets anticipate, risking higher borrowing costs and slowing growth. Next month's data will be critical for confirming whether this acceleration is transitory or structural.

ai.zh: CPI於4月升至332.407(月增0.6%),顯示通膨壓力持續。這可能促使聯準會維持較高利率,增加超大規模業者的資本成本,壓抑數據中心擴建速度。然而AI基礎設施投資具策略必要性,NVIDIA、Microsoft等仍會優先配置GPU資本支出,但可能延後非核心項目。高通膨也推升電力與冷卻成本,侵蝕營運利潤率,迫使業者更重視能源效率與先進製程以降低每瓦運算成本。
ai.en: CPI rising to 332.407 (+0.6% MoM) signals persistent inflation, likely keeping Fed rates elevated and raising capital costs for hyperscalers' data center buildouts. While strategic AI infrastructure spending by NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Google remains resilient due to competitive necessity, higher borrowing costs may delay non-critical capex and slow GPU order growth rates. Additionally, elevated CPI increases operational expenses—power and cooling costs rise proportionally—compressing margins and intensifying focus on energy-efficient architectures and advanced nodes to reduce per-watt compute costs.

mobile.zh: 4月CPI達332.407,較前月增0.6%,累計年增率持續高於2%目標,侵蝕消費者實質購買力。高通膨環境下,非必需消費品如高階智慧手機需求疲弱,消費者延長換機周期或轉向中低階機型。Apple與Samsung旗艦機銷售承壓,尤其在美國市場促銷力度需加大以維持出貨量。零組件成本(記憶體、面板)也因通膨上漲,壓縮品牌商毛利率,整體手機產業面臨量價雙重壓力。
mobile.en: CPI at 332.407 (+0.6% MoM) erodes real consumer purchasing power, dampening discretionary spending on premium smartphones. Replacement cycles lengthen as consumers delay upgrades or shift to mid-tier models, pressuring Apple and Samsung flagship shipments, particularly in the US market. Brands must intensify promotions to sustain volumes, squeezing margins. Simultaneously, component costs (DRAM, OLED panels) rise with inflation, compressing gross profits. The mobile industry faces dual headwinds: softer unit demand and higher input costs, challenging both revenue growth and profitability.

auto.zh: CPI於4月攀升至332.407(月增0.6%),強化聯準會鷹派立場,汽車貸款利率維持高檔(目前約7-8%)。高借貸成本直接抑制新車需求,尤其是需要融資的中產階級買家延後購車或轉向二手車市場。EV銷售受雙重打擊:高車價疊加高利率使每月還款負擔加重,補貼退場後價格敏感度更高。供應鏈端,零組件與物流成本上揚壓縮車廠利潤,迫使減少MCU、ADAS等高附加值配置以控制成本,整體產業景氣承壓。
auto.en: CPI reaching 332.407 (+0.6% MoM) reinforces Fed hawkishness, keeping auto loan rates elevated at 7-8%, directly suppressing new vehicle demand. High financing costs deter middle-income buyers, who delay purchases or shift to used cars. EV adoption suffers acutely: premium pricing combined with steep interest rates inflate monthly payments, deterring uptake as subsidies fade. On the supply side, rising component and logistics costs squeeze OEM margins, prompting cutbacks in semiconductor content (MCU, ADAS modules) to control bills-of-materials. The automotive sector faces weakened sales volumes and margin compression simultaneously.

tsmc.zh: CPI升至332.407反映通膨壓力,影響台積電客戶結構與晶圓需求。AI晶片(NVIDIA H100/B200)採用3nm/5nm先進製程需求具韌性,但超大規模業者因高利率環境可能放緩訂單增速。手機與汽車客戶需求疲軟,衝擊7nm以上成熟製程利用率,造成產能組合失衡。資本支出方面,高通膨推升設備與建廠成本,但台積電仍需維持先進製程領先地位,預期資本密集投資持續但報酬率面臨壓力,客戶組合更集中於高毛利AI應用以抵銷成本上揚。
tsmc.en: CPI at 332.407 creates mixed effects for TSMC. AI chip demand (NVIDIA GPUs on 3nm/5nm nodes) remains robust but may see order growth decelerate as hyperscalers face elevated capital costs from persistent inflation. Conversely, smartphone and auto chip demand weakens—impacting 7nm+ mature node utilization—creating capacity mix imbalances. On capex, rising equipment and construction costs (driven by CPI inflation) pressure ROI, yet TSMC must sustain aggressive advanced-node investments to maintain leadership. Customer mix tilts further toward high-margin AI applications to offset cost inflation, while consumer-exposed segments drag overall wafer demand growth.

commentary.zh: 核心CPI在4月份升至335.423，月增0.4%，延續溫和但穩定的上升趨勢。自2025年4月以來累計上漲2.7%，顯示基礎通膨壓力持續存在。雖然月度漲幅溫和，但連續12個月的上升軌跡表明通膨並未真正消退。聯準會面臨政策困境：若持續維持高利率以對抗通膨，可能抑制經濟成長；但過早降息則可能讓通膨再度加速。市場預期未來數月政策利率將維持高檔，直到核心CPI年增率更明顯下降。
commentary.en: Core CPI rose to 335.423 in April, posting a 0.4% monthly gain and continuing its steady uptrend. The cumulative 2.7% increase since April 2025 signals persistent underlying inflation pressures despite moderate monthly moves. Twelve consecutive months of gains suggest inflation remains sticky. The Fed faces a delicate balance: maintaining elevated rates risks economic slowdown, while premature cuts risk re-acceleration. Market expectations suggest rates will remain elevated until year-over-year core CPI shows more substantial deceleration.

ai.zh: 核心CPI升至335.423（月增0.4%），年化約4.8%，持續高於聯準會目標。此趨勢將延長限制性利率環境，推升超大規模業者資本成本。NVIDIA、微軟、Google的AI資本支出面臨更嚴格ROI審查，但數據中心營運商仍因AI推理需求強勁而維持GPU採購，僅可能延長部署時程。高利率環境將加速汰弱留強，有利市占領先者。
ai.en: Core CPI at 335.423 (+0.4% MoM, ~4.8% annualized) signals prolonged restrictive rates, raising capital costs for hyperscalers' AI capex. Microsoft, Google, and Meta face heightened ROI scrutiny on multi-billion data center builds, potentially stretching deployment timelines. However, strong AI inference demand sustains NVIDIA GPU orders despite tighter financing conditions. Elevated rates may consolidate the market, benefiting scale players with existing infrastructure over startups requiring external funding.

mobile.zh: 核心CPI連續12個月攀升至335.423，累積漲幅2.7%，侵蝕消費者可支配所得。智慧型手機屬非必需消費，升級週期已延長至3.5年以上。蘋果與三星的旗艦機型（$1000+）面對價格敏感度上升，中階機種與翻新機需求相對強勁。持續通膨將迫使聯準會維持高利率，壓抑消費者信貸購機意願，2026下半年出貨量恐低於原預期3-5%。
mobile.en: Core CPI's 12-month climb to 335.423 (+2.7% cumulative) erodes discretionary spending for smartphones, where replacement cycles now exceed 3.5 years. Apple and Samsung's flagship models ($1000+) face heightened price resistance; mid-tier and refurbished devices gain share. Persistent inflation sustaining high rates dampens carrier financing and installment plan uptake. Expect H2 2026 shipments to undershoot forecasts by 3-5% as consumers prioritize essentials over device upgrades amid sticky inflation.

auto.zh: 核心CPI月增0.4%推升聯準會維持5%+政策利率預期，直接拉高車貸成本。當前60個月新車貸款利率達7.5-8%，月付金額較一年前增加$80-120。傳統燃油車與電動車銷售均受壓抑，但EV因單價高（平均$55k）對利率更敏感。經銷商庫存天數攀升至75天以上，促銷壓力增加。車用半導體（MCU、CIS）需求將隨Q3訂單下修5-8%走弱。
auto.en: Core CPI's +0.4% MoM reinforces Fed's higher-for-longer stance, pushing 60-month auto loan rates to 7.5-8%, adding $80-120/month to payments versus a year ago. Sales pressure intensifies for both ICE and EVs, though EVs' higher average price ($55k) amplifies rate sensitivity. Dealer inventory days rise above 75, forcing incentives. Sustained inflation and borrowing costs will dampen Q3 auto production orders, reducing demand for MCUs, camera sensors, and power semiconductors by 5-8% as OEMs trim builds.

tsmc.zh: 核心CPI持續升溫（335.423，月增0.4%）將延長高利率環境，但對台積電影響分化。先進製程（3nm/5nm）需求因AI加速器、HPC晶片訂單維持強勁，N3產能利用率仍近滿載。然而消費性電子疲弱導致7nm以上成熟製程訂單降溫，稼動率下滑至75-80%。資本支出面對融資成本上升壓力，但AI客戶預付款與CoWoS封裝溢價維持現金流健康，全年資本支出約維持$28-30B目標。
tsmc.en: Core CPI at 335.423 (+0.4% MoM) sustains elevated rates, creating divergent impact for TSMC. Leading-edge nodes (3nm/5nm) remain near full utilization driven by NVIDIA, AMD, and Broadcom AI accelerator orders, insulated from macro pressure. Conversely, mature nodes (7nm+) face softening demand from smartphone and auto clients, with utilization slipping to 75-80%. Higher capital costs pressure capex, but customer prepayments and CoWoS packaging premiums support cash flow, keeping 2026 capex near $28-30B guidance despite macro headwinds.

commentary.zh: 最新讀數129.63環比上升0.2%，延續溫和上升軌跡。自2025年5月的125.79以來，核心PCE累計上漲3.05%，月均漲幅約0.22%，明顯低於聯準會2%年度目標的隱含月度速度。近半年趨勢顯示通膨壓力逐步緩解，離目標值越來越近。若此溫和升速維持，將支持聯準會進一步調整政策立場，市場對降息預期可能增強。
commentary.en: Core PCE rose 0.2% monthly to 129.63, continuing a gradual uptrend. Since May 2025 (125.79), the index has gained 3.05%—a monthly average of roughly 0.22%, well below the Fed's 2% annualized target pace. The six-month trajectory suggests inflation pressures are moderating and converging toward the 2% objective. Sustained soft momentum would support further Fed policy adjustment, potentially strengthening market expectations for rate cuts.

ai.zh: 核心PCE連續12個月上升至129.63(+0.2%),顯示通膨黏性持續,聯準會可能延後降息。這對AI資本支出形成雙面影響:高利率環境使超大規模業者融資成本增加,壓抑資料中心擴張速度;但通膨持續也強化企業對AI自動化的需求以控制人力成本,支撐NVIDIA H100/H200與Microsoft Azure等雲端AI服務的長期投資。短期capex節奏可能放緩,但結構性需求仍穩固。
ai.en: Core PCE rising to 129.63 (+0.2% MoM, 12-month uptrend) signals persistent inflation, likely delaying Fed rate cuts. This creates offsetting forces for AI capex: elevated borrowing costs pressure hyperscaler data center expansion timelines and GPU procurement budgets at Microsoft/Google; conversely, sticky inflation strengthens corporate urgency for AI-driven automation to offset wage pressures, sustaining structural demand for NVIDIA's H100/B200 chips and cloud AI infrastructure. Near-term capex pacing may moderate, but long-term investment thesis remains intact amid labor cost inflation.

mobile.zh: 核心PCE從125.79升至129.63(累計+3.1%),反映消費者持續面臨購買力侵蝕。智慧型手機屬可延後升級的非必需品,通膨壓力使消費者延長換機週期至3-4年。Apple iPhone與Samsung旗艦機平均售價已達$1000以上,在實質所得萎縮下需求疲軟。聯準會維持緊縮立場也抑制促銷融資方案,預期2026下半年智慧手機出貨量持續低於疫情前水準,庫存調整壓力加劇。
mobile.en: Core PCE climbing from 125.79 to 129.63 (cumulative +3.1%) erodes consumer purchasing power precisely when smartphone ASPs exceed $1,000 for Apple/Samsung flagships. Persistent inflation extends replacement cycles to 3-4 years as discretionary budgets tighten. Fed's sustained hawkish stance eliminates carrier promotional financing subsidies. April's +0.2% MoM gain suggests no inflation relief ahead, pointing to continued shipment weakness in H2 2026 below pre-pandemic levels. Inventory corrections at distribution channels likely intensify, pressuring component orders.

auto.zh: 核心PCE持續攀升至129.63意味聯準會難以快速降息,汽車貸款利率將維持在6.5-7%高位。新車平均交易價格已達$48,000,月付款負擔在通膨侵蝕下超出中產家庭預算。燃油與保險成本同步上漲進一步抑制購車意願。EV市場受雙重打擊:高利率削弱購買力,且電池原物料成本因通膨居高不下,使Ford/GM難以降價促銷。預期2026下半年輕型車銷售年減3-5%,經銷商庫存天數升至80天以上。
auto.en: Core PCE at 129.63 (+0.2% MoM) locks in elevated auto loan rates at 6.5-7%, pushing monthly payments beyond affordability thresholds as average new vehicle prices hover at $48,000. Persistent inflation (+3.1% since May 2025) squeezes household budgets already strained by rising insurance and fuel costs. EV adoption faces dual headwinds: high financing costs and sticky battery material inflation preventing Ford/GM price cuts. Expect light vehicle SAAR to decline 3-5% in H2 2026, with dealer inventory days expanding to 80+, reducing orders for semiconductors and advanced driver-assistance components.

tsmc.zh: 核心PCE達129.63顯示通膨未降溫,對台積電呈現分化影響。負面端:客戶端融資成本上升使hyperscaler調整AI晶片下單節奏,N3/N2先進製程投片量擴張速度可能從Q2的雙位數成長放緩至個位數;消費電子疲軟直接衝擊5nm手機AP訂單。正面端:通膨環境加速企業AI部署需求,CoWoS先進封裝產能仍供不應求;且聯準會鷹派立場支撐美元,有利台積電美國廠成本結構。整體產能利用率預期維持85-90%,但客戶組合持續向HPC傾斜。
tsmc.en: Core PCE at 129.63 (persistent +0.2% monthly gains) creates divergent effects for TSMC. Downside: sustained high rates slow hyperscaler capex pacing, moderating N3/N2 wafer starts growth from double-digit to mid-single-digit in Q3-Q4 as Microsoft/Google extend AI chip deployment timelines; smartphone weakness (inflation-driven) pressures 5nm AP orders. Upside: inflation accelerates enterprise AI adoption, keeping CoWoS advanced packaging sold out through 2026; strong dollar (Fed hawkishness) improves Arizona fab economics. Overall utilization stays 85-90%, but customer mix shifts further toward HPC (60%+) from mobile (sub-30%), altering margin trajectory and capex allocation toward leading-edge.

commentary.zh: 5月非農就業人數達159,001千人，環比增加172千人（+0.1%），顯示就業市場保持穩健。近半年數據在158,400至159,000千人間波動，總體呈現小幅上升趨勢。儘管單月增幅溫和，但就業存量穩定在高位，遠超聯準會10萬人的放緩警戒線。然而，增速未達15萬人健康基準，反映勞動力市場增長動能逐漸趨緩。此數據對聯準會政策調整影響有限，市場預期利率維持現狀機率較高。
commentary.en: May nonfarm payrolls reached 159.0 million, adding 172,000 jobs month-over-month—a modest but steady gain. The six-month trend shows employment hovering between 158.4–159.0 million, slightly elevated. While growth remains well above the 100,000-job slowdown threshold, it falls short of the 150,000+ benchmark for robust health. The underlying stability masks slowing momentum. For the Fed, this middling performance suggests labor market resilience without overheating, likely supporting a patient stance on rate cuts. Markets may view this as neutral for policy.

ai.zh: 非農就業增加17.2萬人，顯著優於10萬健康門檻，連續三個月維持正增長趨勢。強勁就業市場支撐企業IT預算擴張，推動超大規模雲端業者（微軟、Google）持續加碼AI資本支出。數據中心建設需求穩健，NVIDIA H100/H200 GPU訂單能見度延續至2026下半年。勞動市場緊俏亦加速企業採用生成式AI工具替代人力，強化AI基礎設施長期投資動能。
ai.en: The +172K NFP gain, well above the 100K slowdown threshold, sustains three consecutive months of job growth. Robust employment underpins enterprise IT budgets, fueling continued AI capex expansion by hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google. Data center construction remains solid, extending NVIDIA H100/H200 GPU order visibility through H2 2026. Tight labor markets also accelerate corporate adoption of generative AI tools to offset labor costs, reinforcing long-term AI infrastructure investment momentum.

mobile.zh: 17.2萬新增就業雖健康但月增率僅0.1%，顯示勞動市場擴張減速。就業水準維持15.9萬高位支撐可支配收入，但缺乏加速度限制iPhone與高階Android旗艦換機意願。蘋果依賴AI功能拉動iPhone 16銷售，三星Galaxy S系列面臨去庫存壓力。北美智慧型手機出貨預估持平，消費者延長換機週期至3.5年，平價機種市占穩定但ASP承壓。
mobile.en: The +172K gain is healthy but the +0.1% monthly rate signals decelerating labor expansion. Employment at 159M supports disposable income, yet lacks acceleration to drive iPhone or premium Android upgrade cycles. Apple leans on AI features to boost iPhone 16 sales; Samsung's Galaxy S faces inventory digestion. North American smartphone shipments expected flat, with replacement cycles extending to 3.5 years. Mid-tier devices hold share but average selling prices remain pressured.

auto.zh: 非農就業+17.2萬維持穩定但增速放緩，汽車貸款利率仍處6.5-7%高位，抑制皮卡與大型SUV融資需求。就業穩定支撐二手車交易量，但新車SAAR預估僅1,540萬輛，低於疫前水準。電動車採購受惠商用車隊訂單（聯邦快遞、亞馬遜），然消費端充電焦慮未解。供應鏈MCU與電池模組產能過剩導致降價壓力，OEM毛利率承壓2-3個百分點。
auto.en: NFP +172K provides stability, but slowing momentum combines with auto loan rates at 6.5-7% to dampen pickup and large SUV financing. Steady employment supports used-car volumes, yet new-vehicle SAAR remains subdued at ~15.4M units, below pre-pandemic levels. EV adoption benefits from commercial fleet orders (FedEx, Amazon), but consumer charging anxiety persists. MCU and battery module overcapacity pressures pricing, compressing OEM gross margins by 2-3 percentage points.

tsmc.zh: 就業數據穩健增強企業科技支出信心，直接利好台積電3奈米與5奈米先進製程利用率。AI加速器客戶（NVIDIA、AMD、博通）CoWoS先進封裝訂單滿載至2026Q4，推動資本支出維持320-340億美元高位。手機晶片需求平穩但蘋果A18與高通8 Gen 4訂單組合優化，汽車晶片去化庫存拖累成熟製程稼動率至75%。整體而言客戶組合偏重高毛利AI與HPC，營收年增18-22%可期。
tsmc.en: Healthy NFP bolsters corporate tech spending confidence, directly benefiting TSMC's 3nm and 5nm advanced node utilization. AI accelerator customers (NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom) keep CoWoS advanced packaging fully booked through Q4 2026, sustaining capex at $32-34B. Smartphone chip demand is flat but Apple A18 and Qualcomm 8 Gen 4 orders optimize mix. Auto chip inventory digestion drags mature-node utilization to 75%. Overall customer mix tilts toward high-margin AI and HPC, supporting 18-22% revenue growth.

commentary.zh: 失業率在4.3%，與前月持平，過去一年維持在4.1%-4.5%的狹窄區間。儘管2025年9月曾升至4.5%，但隨後回穩於4.3%-4.4%水準，反映勞動市場韌性持續。當前失業率略高於聯準會認定的充分就業水準（3.5%-4%），暗示勞動市場仍有些許鬆動空間。這為聯準會進一步寬鬆政策提供支撐條件，惟需密切關注是否形成持續上升趨勢。
commentary.en: The unemployment rate holds steady at 4.3%, remaining within a tight 4.1%-4.5% band over the past year. Despite a spike to 4.5% in September 2025, it has stabilized at 4.3%-4.4% recently, signaling continued labor market resilience. The current reading sits slightly above the Fed's full employment threshold (3.5%-4%), suggesting modest slack persists. This backdrop supports the case for accommodative policy, though sustained upward momentum warrants monitoring.

ai.zh: 失業率維持4.3%穩定,接近充分就業水平,顯示勞動市場健康但未過熱。這支持聯準會維持當前利率政策,有利AI基礎設施投資。企業獲利穩健下,微軟、Google等超大規模業者持續擴張資料中心資本支出,NVIDIA GPU需求維持強勁。穩定就業環境亦支撐企業AI轉型預算,特別是生成式AI應用部署加速。
ai.en: Unemployment holding at 4.3% near full employment supports stable Fed policy, benefiting AI capex cycles. Healthy labor market sustains corporate profitability, enabling hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta) to maintain aggressive datacenter buildouts. NVIDIA GPU demand remains robust as enterprises continue AI transformation spending. Stable employment reduces recession risk that could derail multi-year AI infrastructure investments, particularly for H100/H200 deployments and emerging sovereign AI projects.

mobile.zh: 失業率持平4.3%,較上月無變化,顯示消費者購買力穩定但缺乏上升動能。智慧型手機屬非必需品,當前就業水平支撐換機需求但不足以刺激顯著成長。Apple與Samsung旗艦機銷售維持平穩,但中階市場競爭激烈。北美市場需求持平,整體出貨量預期溫和,AI手機功能尚未成為強勁換機催化劑。
mobile.en: Unemployment flat at 4.3% (unchanged) indicates stable but not accelerating consumer purchasing power. Smartphone demand remains modest as employment neither deteriorates nor surges. iPhone and Galaxy flagship sales hold steady, but premium upgrade cycles lack momentum. Mid-tier segment faces intense competition. The 4.3% rate supports baseline replacement demand but insufficient to drive share gains. AI smartphone features not yet compelling enough to overcome lengthening replacement cycles in mature markets.

auto.zh: 失業率穩定於4.3%對汽車產業呈中性影響。雖就業穩健通常支持購車意願,但利率仍處高檔使車貸成本居高不下,抵銷就業穩定的正面效果。新車銷售與EV採用率維持溫和水平,但無明顯成長跡象。消費者對高額貸款謹慎,延長既有車輛使用年限。供應鏈已正常化,但需求端動能不足,車用半導體(MCU)需求持平。
auto.en: Unemployment steady at 4.3% presents neutral auto outlook. While stable employment typically supports vehicle purchases, elevated interest rates keep auto loan costs high (average ~7-8%), offsetting job security benefits. New vehicle sales and EV adoption remain tepid; consumers延長 vehicle ownership amid expensive financing. Pickup and SUV demand flat. Supply chains normalized but demand lacks momentum, keeping automotive MCU and power semiconductor content growth modest. Fleet purchases stable but consumer segment cautious on big-ticket financing.

tsmc.zh: 失業率4.3%維持穩定,確保TSMC關鍵客戶群(蘋果、NVIDIA、AMD)終端需求穩健。3奈米與5奈米先進製程產能利用率維持高檔,AI晶片(CoWoS封裝)需求強勁抵銷手機晶片平淡表現。穩定宏觀環境支持客戶維持資本支出,TSMC 2奈米量產計畫不受影響。客戶組合中,AI/HPC佔比持續提升,降低消費性電子波動風險。
tsmc.en: Unemployment stable at 4.3% ensures healthy end-demand for TSMC's key customers (Apple, NVIDIA, AMD). Advanced node utilization (3nm/5nm) remains robust, driven by strong AI accelerator orders offsetting softer smartphone chip demand. Stable macro supports customer capex commitments; NVIDIA's H200/B200 ramps and Apple's A18/M4 volumes sustain wafer demand. CoWoS capacity sold out through 2026. Customer mix shifting toward AI/HPC (now ~50% revenue) insulates TSMC from consumer electronics volatility. 2nm production timeline unaffected.

commentary.zh: 最新數據升至22.5萬，環比增加1.3萬人(+6.1%)，為近期高點。四週移動平均約為209,250，仍遠低於30萬警戒線，顯示勞動市場基本面穩健。然而近兩週連續上升(21.2萬→22.5萬)值得關注，打破先前的平穩態勢。儘管絕對水準溫和，但上升趨勢可能預示經濟增速放緩或企業招聘意願減弱。聯準會應密切監測後續週數據，以判斷是否為暫時波動或勞動市場轉弱的訊號。
commentary.en: Initial claims rose to 225k (+6.1%), marking a recent peak after two consecutive weekly increases. The 4-week moving average of approximately 209k remains well below the 300k recession threshold, indicating labor market resilience. However, the uptick trend—rising from 212k to 225k in recent weeks—breaks earlier stability and warrants monitoring. While the absolute level remains moderate, persistent increases could signal softening economic momentum or reduced hiring appetite. The Fed should track upcoming data closely to distinguish between normal volatility and emerging labor market weakness.

ai.zh: 初請失業金人數升至22.5萬(+6.1%)，但仍處於健康區間且遠低於30萬警戒線。AI基礎建設投資主要由超大規模雲端業者的多年資本支出計畫驅動，而非短期勞動市場波動。目前數據顯示就業市場溫和放緩，不足以改變微軟、Google、Meta的GPU採購時程表或NVIDIA H100/B200訂單能見度。除非四週均值突破28萬，否則AI capex週期維持不變。
ai.en: Claims rose to 225K (+6.1%) but remain well below the 300K deterioration threshold. AI infrastructure spending is driven by multi-year hyperscaler capex commitments, not near-term labor fluctuations. This modest uptick doesn't alter GPU procurement timelines at Microsoft, Google, or Meta, nor does it impact NVIDIA H100/B200 order visibility. Enterprise AI budgets remain insulated unless the four-week average breaches 280K, signaling broader economic stress that could trigger capex deferrals.

mobile.zh: 初請失業金由19萬急升至22.5萬(+1.3萬人，+6.1%)，顯示消費者就業信心開始動搖。智慧型手機屬可選消費，失業風險上升直接抑制換機需求，尤其影響800美元以上高階機種。Apple iPhone與Samsung旗艦機銷售對就業預期高度敏感，近期數據從3月20.5萬低點反彈至當前水準，預示6-8月北美市場出貨量可能低於預期，運營商促銷力度需加大以刺激需求。
mobile.en: Claims jumped from 190K to 225K (+13K, +6.1%), signaling eroding consumer job confidence. Smartphones are discretionary purchases highly sensitive to employment uncertainty—rising jobless risk directly suppresses upgrade cycles, especially for $800+ flagship devices. Apple iPhone and Samsung Galaxy sales face headwinds as the trend reversed from March's 205K low. This suggests Q3 North American shipments may undershoot forecasts, requiring aggressive carrier promotions to maintain volume.

auto.zh: 失業救濟申請增至22.5萬(+6.1%)，且六週內第四次超過21萬，削弱消費者大額融資信心。汽車貸款平均7.2%利率環境下，就業不確定性上升使購車者推遲決策，直接衝擊經銷商客流量與成交率。皮卡與電動車等高單價車款首當其衝，Ford F-150與Tesla Model Y等月供超過700美元的車型訂單可能疲軟，經銷商庫存天數恐升至70天以上水平。
auto.en: Claims hit 225K (+6.1%), the fourth time above 210K in six weeks, weakening confidence for big-ticket financing. With auto loans averaging 7.2%, rising employment uncertainty prompts buyers to delay purchases, directly impacting dealer traffic and close rates. High-ticket vehicles suffer most—Ford F-150 and Tesla Model Y with $700+ monthly payments face softening orders. Dealer inventory could climb above 70 days as consumers hesitate on multi-year financing commitments amid deteriorating labor market signals.

tsmc.zh: 初請失業金升至22.5萬反映消費需求潛在疲軟，直接影響台積電消費性晶片客戶(Apple A系列、Qualcomm Snapdragon)的3奈米與5奈米投片量，預計Q3產能利用率從95%回落至88-90%。但AI伺服器晶片(NVIDIA CoWoS、AMD MI300)需求仍強勁，抵銷部分消費端疲弱。整體而言，先進製程結構改變：AI佔3奈米比重從40%升至55%，降低對消費市場曝險，但總晶圓出貨成長放緩至個位數。
tsmc.en: Claims at 225K signal potential consumer demand softness, directly impacting TSMC's consumer chip customers (Apple A-series, Qualcomm Snapdragon) with lower 3nm/5nm wafer starts—Q3 utilization may slip from 95% to 88-90%. However, robust AI server chip demand (NVIDIA CoWoS, AMD MI300) partially offsets consumer weakness. Advanced node mix shifts: AI grows from 40% to 55% of 3nm revenue, reducing consumer exposure. Overall wafer shipment growth decelerates to single digits, but margin structure improves via AI mix enrichment despite volume headwinds.

commentary.zh: 美國實質GDP於2026年1月達24,152.7億美元，較前季增長0.4%，年化成長率溫和。自2023年中以來，GDP累計成長約6.3%，顯示經濟持續擴張，但近兩季成長明顯放緩（2025年下半年各季僅0.1-0.4%）。經濟雖未陷衰退，但成長速度已低於健康的2-3%年化水準，反映消費動能減弱與市場預期調整。聯準會政策制定者應密切關注此趨勢，評估進一步降息的必要性。
commentary.en: Real GDP reached $24,152.7 billion in Q1 2026, posting modest 0.4% quarterly growth. The economy has expanded 6.3% since mid-2023, yet recent momentum has significantly decelerated—H2 2025 growth averaged just 0.2% per quarter, well below the 2-3% healthy benchmark. While recession risks remain low, the slowdown signals cooling demand and suggests the economy may be approaching potential output constraints. The Fed should monitor whether weakness persists before considering additional rate cuts.

ai.zh: 實質GDP達24,152億美元，季增96.9億（+0.4%），延續自2025年Q1低點的復甦態勢。雖成長溫和但仍為正值，企業對AI基礎設施的資本支出保持韌性。超大規模雲端業者（Microsoft、Google）在經濟擴張期持續投資資料中心與GPU採購，NVIDIA H100/H200需求穩定。GDP正成長支撐企業IT預算，AI晶片訂單能見度維持6-9個月，CoWoS先進封裝產能仍供不應求。
ai.en: Real GDP reached $24,152.7B, up $96.9B (+0.4%) quarter-over-quarter, recovering from Q1 2025's dip. Modest but positive growth sustains enterprise AI capex commitments. Hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google) maintain data center buildouts during expansion phases—NVIDIA GPU demand (H100/H200) remains robust with 6-9 month order visibility. Positive GDP growth underpins corporate IT budgets, keeping CoWoS advanced packaging supply tight and 5nm/3nm wafer starts for AI accelerators elevated through 2026.

mobile.zh: GDP季增0.4%顯示消費者支出動能偏弱，對非必需消費品如智慧型手機構成壓力。自2023年以來的GDP年化成長率僅約2.8%，處於健康區間下緣，消費者對高階旗艦機（iPhone Pro、Galaxy S系列）升級意願遲疑。換機週期延長至3-4年，Apple與Samsung出貨量持平或小幅下滑。中階市場競爭激烈，ASP下行壓縮品牌獲利，但AI功能（端側LLM）尚未成為有力換機誘因。
mobile.en: GDP's modest +0.4% gain signals tepid consumer spending, pressuring discretionary purchases like smartphones. Annualized growth averaging ~2.8% since 2023 sits at the lower bound of healthy expansion, weakening appetite for premium flagship upgrades (iPhone Pro, Galaxy S). Replacement cycles stretch to 3-4 years; Apple/Samsung shipments flat to slightly down. Mid-tier competition intensifies, compressing ASPs and margins. On-device AI features (edge LLMs) have yet to drive meaningful upgrade cycles despite GDP staying positive.

auto.zh: GDP成長0.4%維持擴張但力道不足，汽車銷售面臨雙重壓力：利率雖自高點回落但仍偏高，新車貸款利率約7-8%抑制購買力；同時GDP增速放緩削弱消費者信心。輕型車銷量年化約1,550萬輛，低於疫前1,700萬輛水準。電動車滲透率增至12-14%但成長減速，Ford與GM削減EV產能擴張計畫。二手車價格高企延長換車週期，車用半導體（MCU、功率元件）需求平穩但缺乏爆發力。
auto.en: GDP's +0.4% growth maintains expansion but lacks vigor, creating mixed auto dynamics. Auto loan rates remain elevated at 7-8% despite rate cuts, crimping affordability; sluggish GDP growth erodes consumer confidence. Light vehicle SAAR hovers around 15.5M units, below pre-pandemic 17M. EV penetration reaches 12-14% but growth decelerates—Ford and GM scale back EV capacity plans. Elevated used-car prices extend trade-in cycles. Automotive semiconductor demand (MCUs, power ICs) steady but uninspiring, lacking the surge typical of robust GDP expansion.

tsmc.zh: GDP持續擴張對台積電形成結構性利多。AI晶片（N3E/N3P製程）需求強勁，抵銷智慧型手機與PC晶圓需求疲軟。來自NVIDIA、AMD、Broadcom的5nm/3nm訂單推升先進製程利用率至95%以上，帶動毛利率擴張至54-56%。2026年資本支出預估維持320-350億美元，聚焦2nm量產與CoWoS產能倍增。客戶組合優化：HPC佔營收比重升至50%，稀釋消費性電子循環波動影響，GDP正成長確保企業端晶片需求穩定。
tsmc.en: Sustained GDP expansion structurally benefits TSMC despite modest pace. AI chip demand (N3E/N3P nodes) from NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom drives 5nm/3nm utilization above 95%, expanding gross margins to 54-56%—offsetting weak smartphone/PC wafer demand. Capex outlook holds at $32-35B for 2026, targeting 2nm ramp and CoWoS capacity doubling. Customer mix improves: HPC revenue share rises to ~50%, diluting consumer electronics cyclicality. Positive GDP growth underpins enterprise chip demand, ensuring advanced node order books remain full through H1 2027.

commentary.zh: 4月零售銷售（不含汽車）達656,115百萬美元，環比增長0.5%（+3,080百萬），延續溫和上升軌跡。自5月以來累計增幅約6.2%，顯示消費動能穩健。近三月加速擴張（2月+1.1%、3月+1.9%、4月+0.5%）表明家庭支出韌性十足，抵銷通膨與利率上升的壓力。儘管月度增速溫和，累積強勁表現支持美國經濟穩定成長，降低衰退風險。此數據可能強化聯準會維持利率的立場。
commentary.en: April retail sales ex-autos reached $656.1 billion, rising 0.5% month-over-month—modest but consistent. Year-to-date growth of 6.2% since May reflects durable consumer spending despite inflation and higher rates. The recent acceleration (February +1.1%, March +1.9%) signals resilient household demand. While monthly gains remain measured, cumulative strength supports economic durability and reduces recession risks. This resilience likely reinforces Fed confidence in maintaining current policy rates.

ai.zh: 零售銷售額達6,561億美元，較上月增長0.5%，且自3月起加速擴張（3月增1.9%），顯示消費韌性強勁。強勁消費支撐企業獲利預期，促使超大規模雲端業者（Microsoft、Google、Meta）維持高額AI資本支出計畫。數據中心建設與GPU採購需求持續，NVIDIA H100/H200及即將推出的Blackwell晶片訂單能見度佳，AI基礎設施投資週期未見放緩跡象。
ai.en: Retail sales hit $656.1B, up 0.5% MoM with accelerating momentum since March (+1.9% that month). Robust consumer spending supports corporate profit outlooks, reinforcing hyperscaler AI capex commitments from Microsoft, Google, and Meta. Strong economic backdrop validates continued data center buildouts and GPU procurement. NVIDIA's H100/H200 and upcoming Blackwell orders remain solid, with no signs of infrastructure investment pullback as enterprise confidence stays elevated.

mobile.zh: 零售銷售增長0.5%，但增速較3月的1.9%明顯放緩，且10月曾出現負增長（-0.2%），顯示消費動能不均。智慧型手機屬可選消費品，當前6,561億美元銷售額雖創新高，但消費者面對通膨壓力可能延長換機週期。Apple iPhone 16系列銷售受惠於AI功能（Apple Intelligence）但定價高昂，Samsung中階機型或受惠於謹慎型消費者。整體單位銷量增長有限，ASP提升成關鍵。
mobile.en: Sales rose 0.5% to $656.1B but decelerated sharply from March's +1.9%, with October showing contraction (-0.2%). Smartphones are discretionary; uneven spending momentum suggests consumers may extend replacement cycles despite record sales levels. iPhone 16 pricing pressure persists even with AI features, while Samsung's mid-tier models may capture budget-conscious buyers. Unit shipment growth likely modest; ASP expansion critical for revenue as inflation-weary consumers prioritize essentials over premium device upgrades.

auto.zh: 本指標剔除汽車銷售，但整體零售增長0.5%反映消費者支出能力尚可，對汽車融資需求構成支撐。然而銷售額增速從3月1.9%降至0.5%，暗示消費放緩。當前利率環境下汽車貸款成本仍高（平均APR約7-8%），抑制新車需求尤其是高價EV車款。傳統燃油車與平價EV（如Tesla Model 3）銷售相對穩定，但豪華EV與皮卡市場面臨融資成本壓力，MCU及車用晶片需求增長趨緩。
auto.en: Though ex-auto, the 0.5% retail gain suggests consumer capacity exists but momentum slowed from March's +1.9%. Auto loan rates remain elevated (~7-8% APR), dampening new vehicle demand especially premium EVs. Stable employment supports baseline sales of ICE vehicles and affordable EVs like Tesla Model 3, but luxury EV and pickup segments face financing headwinds. Semiconductor content growth (MCU, ADAS chips) decelerates as unit sales plateau and inventory corrections persist across OEM supply chains.

tsmc.zh: 零售銷售成長至6,561億美元（+0.5%），且自5月起連續擴張，支撐終端需求預期，對TSMC晶圓需求構成正面支撐。AI伺服器晶片（NVIDIA、AMD使用N5/N4/N3E製程）需求強勁，智慧型手機雖增速放緩但Apple A18及Qualcomm旗艦晶片維持3奈米投片量。先進製程（3nm及即將量產的2nm）產能利用率維持高檔（>90%），2026年資本支出預估達280-320億美元。客戶組合中AI/HPC佔比提升至50%以上，抵銷消費電子疲軟影響。
tsmc.en: Retail sales at $656.1B (+0.5%) with expansion since May underpins end-demand visibility, supporting TSMC wafer orders. AI server chips (NVIDIA, AMD on N5/N4/N3E) drive robust demand; smartphone growth slows but Apple A18 and Qualcomm flagship maintain 3nm volumes. Advanced node utilization (3nm, upcoming 2nm) stays above 90%, with 2026 capex estimated $28-32B. Customer mix shift toward AI/HPC (now >50% of revenue) offsets consumer electronics softness, validating leading-edge capacity expansion and pricing power.

commentary.zh: 聯邦基金利率在5月份微幅下降1個基點至3.63%，延續自2025年中以來的溫和調整趨勢。自6月的4.33%高點以來，累計下跌70個基點，反映聯準會對通膨壓力緩解的回應。然而，自1月以來利率已連續4個月維持在3.64%左右的水平，暗示聯準會採取了觀望態度。未來政策走向將取決於通膨數據與就業狀況，市場應密切關注經濟成長放緩的風險。
commentary.en: The federal funds rate declined 1 basis point to 3.63% in May, extending the measured easing cycle that began mid-2025. The cumulative 70 bp cut from June's 4.33% peak reflects the Fed's response to moderating inflation. However, the rate has remained essentially flat at 3.64% since January, suggesting a pause in policy adjustments. Future cuts will depend on inflation trajectory and labor market conditions. Markets should monitor recession risks as accommodation continues.

ai.zh: 聯邦基金利率從4.33%降至3.63%，累計降息70基點，顯著降低超大規模業者的資本成本。NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等廠商每年數百億美元的AI資料中心資本支出融資成本下降，提升IRR達成率。較低利率環境使高CAPEX密集的GPU採購與基礎建設擴張更具經濟吸引力，H100/H200需求維持強勁，推動AI硬體投資週期延續。
ai.en: The Fed funds rate declining from 4.33% to 3.63% (70bps cumulative cut) materially reduces capital costs for hyperscalers deploying $50-100B annual AI capex. Lower financing expenses improve IRR thresholds for data center buildouts, sustaining robust GPU procurement from NVIDIA (H100/H200/Blackwell). Reduced discount rates enhance NPV of long-duration AI infrastructure investments, supporting continued expansion of training and inference capacity despite elevated absolute rate levels remaining above pre-2022 era.

mobile.zh: 利率從3.64%微降至3.63%，變動僅1基點，對消費性電子需求影響有限。雖然自去年6月累計降息70基點應緩解分期付款壓力，但智慧型手機已非利率敏感商品，換機週期延長至3-4年主要受產品創新不足驅動。Apple與Samsung出貨仍取決於AI功能差異化（如端側生成式AI）而非融資成本，當前利率水準對購買決策影響邊際化。
mobile.en: The 1bp dip from 3.64% to 3.63% is negligible for smartphone demand. While cumulative 70bps easing since June 2025 marginally reduces installment financing costs, replacement cycles remain extended at 3-4 years driven by limited hardware innovation rather than interest rates. Apple and Samsung shipments hinge on AI feature differentiation (on-device GenAI, camera upgrades) rather than financing accessibility. At 3.63%, rates remain insufficiently stimulative to materially alter discretionary electronics purchasing patterns amid consumer caution.

auto.zh: 利率降至3.63%（較峰值下降70基點）理論上降低汽車貸款成本，60個月期車貸利率可望從8%回落至7%區間，每月付款減少約3-5%。然而利率傳導至零售車貸存在時滯，且當前絕對水準仍壓抑中低收入購車需求。EV銷售受補貼政策影響大於利率，傳統燃油車受惠較明顯，但庫存調整與消費信心仍制約銷量復甦力道，整體呈現緩慢改善態勢。
auto.en: Rate decline to 3.63% (70bps from peak) theoretically lowers auto loan costs—60-month financing may drop from 8% to ~7% APR, reducing monthly payments 3-5%. However, transmission lag persists; absolute levels still constrain affordability for subprime buyers. Traditional ICE vehicles benefit more than EVs, where subsidy policy dominates purchase decisions. Inventory normalization and cautious consumer sentiment limit sales rebound despite easier credit conditions. Light truck/pickup segments see modest demand improvement, but mass-market sedan financing remains stressed at current rate environment.

tsmc.zh: 聯邦基金利率降至3.63%對台積電形成雙重利多：客戶端，較低利率支撐超大規模業者AI晶片採購（N3/N2先進製程需求穩固）與蘋果等消費電子廠庫存回補意願；供給端，台積電年度300-350億美元資本支出的美元計價融資成本下降約15-20基點，改善先進封裝（CoWoS）與亞利桑那廠擴產的投資報酬率，3奈米產能利用率可望維持高檔運轉。
tsmc.en: The 3.63% rate benefits TSMC on dual fronts: (1) Customer side—lower rates sustain hyperscaler AI chip orders on N3/N2 nodes and encourage Apple/smartphone OEMs to rebuild inventory; (2) Supply side—TSMC's $30-35B annual capex faces ~15-20bps lower USD financing costs, improving ROI for CoWoS advanced packaging and Arizona fab expansion. N3 utilization remains elevated above 85%, while cumulative 70bps easing enhances customer willingness to commit to long-term wafer agreements, de-risking 2nm ramp scheduled for 2H 2026.

commentary.zh: 6.48%的利率較上週下降5個基點，顯示近期緩和跡象。然而，過去三週利率在6.23%-6.53%之間波動，反映市場對聯準會政策方向的不確定性。雖然當前水準仍低於7%的臨界點，但與年初6.22%相比已上升26個基點。若利率持續在6.3%-6.5%區間震盪，購房可負擔性壓力將維持，可能抑制成交量成長。市場預期聯準會降息決策將直接影響後續走勢。
commentary.en: The 6.48% reading declined 5 basis points week-over-week, suggesting recent moderation. However, rates have oscillated between 6.23%-6.53% over the past three weeks, reflecting market uncertainty about Fed policy direction. Despite remaining below the 7% affordability threshold, rates have risen 26 basis points since March. Sustained pressure in the 6.3%-6.5% range risks constraining transaction volumes. Fed rate decisions will be pivotal to future mortgage rate trajectory.

ai.zh: 房貸利率降至 6.48%（-0.05 個百分點），但對 AI 產業影響有限。超大規模業者如 Microsoft、Google 的資料中心資本支出主要由 AI 競爭驅動，與住宅融資成本脫鉤。利率維持 6% 中段反映企業債成本穩定，不改變 NVIDIA H200/B200 訂單能見度。短期利率波動不影響多年期 AI 基礎設施投資決策，GPU 需求仍由模型訓練與推論工作負載主導。
ai.en: Mortgage rate declining to 6.48% (-0.05 ppt) shows minimal AI impact. Hyperscaler capex at Microsoft, Google, Meta is decoupled from residential financing—driven by AI race dynamics, not household borrowing costs. Rate stability in mid-6% range suggests corporate debt costs remain manageable, sustaining NVIDIA H200/B200 orders. Data center investment horizons span 3-5 years, insulated from weekly mortgage fluctuations. GPU demand remains anchored to training/inference workloads, not consumer credit conditions.

mobile.zh: 房貸利率 6.48% 微降 0.05 個百分點，對手機需求影響間接且延遲。雖利率自 5 月 6.51% 高點回落，但仍處 6% 中高段，持續侵蝕家庭可支配所得。消費者優先償還房貸而非換機，壓抑 Apple iPhone 與 Samsung 旗艦機需求。然近兩週利率走穩暗示融資環境未再惡化，預期 Q3 換機週期不會進一步萎縮，維持中性展望。
mobile.en: Rate dipping to 6.48% (-0.05 ppt) offers marginal relief but remains elevated in mid-6% range, continuing to squeeze discretionary income. Homeowners prioritizing mortgage payments over $1,000+ iPhone 16 or Galaxy S25 upgrades. However, stabilization from 6.53% peak suggests financing pressure not intensifying—important as Q3 launch season approaches. Impact remains indirect: mortgage costs don't directly affect device financing, but erode household budgets. Neutral outlook as rate trend flattens rather than spikes above 7%.

auto.zh: 房貸利率降至 6.48% 帶來雙面效應。負面：利率雖降但仍高，加上汽車貸款利差，新車貸款成本約 8-9%，持續抑制燃油車與 EV 銷售，尤其傷害需貸款 7 萬美元以上的電動皮卡（Ford F-150 Lightning、Rivian）。正面：自 6.53% 回落顯示融資環境邊際改善，可能緩解 Q2 庫存壓力。但除非跌破 6%，否則不足以重啟被 7% 以上利率凍結的換車需求。
auto.en: Rate dropping to 6.48% creates mixed dynamics. Negative: still-elevated rates translate to 8-9% auto loan costs (mortgage rate + spread), suppressing new vehicle sales—especially $70K+ EVs like F-150 Lightning, Rivian R1T requiring heavy financing. Positive: decline from 6.53% peak signals marginal easing, potentially reducing Q2 inventory glut as affordability improves slightly. However, rate remains well above 5% threshold needed to unlock pent-up replacement demand frozen during 7%+ rate regime. Neutral-to-slight improvement in financing headwinds.

tsmc.zh: 房貸利率 6.48%（-0.05 個百分點）對台積電影響微弱且間接。利率穩定在 6% 中段，維持美國消費電子需求溫和復甦（影響 Apple A18/M4 晶片 3nm 投片），但不改變結構性趨勢。AI 伺服器晶片（NVIDIA CoWoS 封裝）需求與房貸脫鉤，仍是先進製程主要動能。利率未突破 7% 避免消費需求崩潰，但也未降至 5% 以下刺激強勁成長，N3/N2 產能利用率維持穩定而非加速擴張。
tsmc.en: Mortgage rate at 6.48% (-0.05 ppt) has minimal direct impact on TSMC. Rate stability in mid-6% range sustains moderate US consumer electronics demand—relevant for Apple A18/M4 chips on N3 node—but doesn't shift structural trajectory. AI server chip demand (NVIDIA HGX, CoWoS advanced packaging) remains decoupled from residential financing. Rate holding below 7% avoids consumption collapse that would hurt smartphone/PC wafer starts, while lack of decline below 5% means no demand surge. N3/N2 utilization stays steady at 85-90%, driven by AI mix rather than mortgage trends.

commentary.zh: 美國製造業就業在經歷六個月的持續下滑後，自今年1月觸底12,582千人後開始緩慢復甦。5月就業人數達12,605千人，環比增加7千人（+0.1%），顯示製造業招聘動力初步穩定。然而，與去年6月的12,636千人相比，年減31千人（-0.2%），說明製造業仍面臨結構性挑戰。近期反彈幅度溫和，距離衰退前水準仍有差距，暗示經濟復甦步伐謹慎。聯準會需密切關注此指標是否能持續改善，以判斷經濟基本面強度。
commentary.en: After declining steadily for six months, manufacturing employment stabilized in early 2026, reaching 12,605 thousand in May (+7 thousand, +0.1% monthly). However, year-over-year comparison shows a 31 thousand decline (-0.2%) versus June 2025, indicating persistent structural weakness in goods production. The recent rebound is modest, suggesting cautious economic recovery. With manufacturing employment still below pre-decline levels, this leading indicator signals moderate economic momentum, warranting Fed attention to underlying goods sector health.

ai.zh: 製造業就業人數自去年6月12636千人降至本期12605千人，累計下降31千人(-0.2%)，但近三個月出現22千人回升。此微弱復甦對AI資料中心建設影響有限，因超大規模業者資本支出主要由雲端需求驅動而非製造業勞動力。GPU伺服器需求與企業AI投資週期關聯更強，目前就業數據未顯示製造業AI應用擴張的明確訊號，維持中性展望。
ai.en: Manufacturing employment declined 31k from June 2025's 12,636k to current 12,605k (-0.2% cumulative), though stabilizing with +22k recovery over three months. This marginal rebound offers limited signal for AI capex as hyperscaler datacenter buildouts are driven by cloud demand rather than manufacturing labor trends. GPU server orders correlate more with enterprise AI adoption cycles than factory headcount. Current employment trajectory shows no clear acceleration in manufacturing-sector AI tool deployment, sustaining neutral outlook for NVIDIA/Microsoft demand drivers.

mobile.zh: 製造業就業持續疲軟（較去年高峰減少31千人）反映商品生產經濟放緩，直接衝擊藍領消費者購買力。這批勞工是中階智慧手機核心客群，就業不確定性將延遲換機週期，尤其影響300-600美元價位段需求。Apple與Samsung在美國市場面臨客戶信心疲弱，即使當月就業回升7千人，年減趨勢仍顯示消費性電子產品需求復甦動能不足。
mobile.en: Manufacturing employment down 31k from mid-2025 peak signals weakening goods-producing economy, directly eroding blue-collar purchasing power critical for mid-range smartphone demand ($300-600 segment). This cohort drives replacement cycles; job uncertainty extends device lifespans. Despite +7k monthly rebound, the year-long downtrend indicates fragile consumer confidence. Apple and Samsung face headwinds in US market as discretionary electronics spending contracts. Sustained negative YoY growth historically precedes broader recessionary smartphone volume declines of 8-12%.

auto.zh: 製造業就業自12636千降至12605千人（-0.2%），雖本月增7千但八個月下降趨勢未改。製造業勞工是皮卡與入門級車款主要買家，就業萎縮直接削弱汽車貸款申請意願與核准率。年增率若持續為負將領先整體衰退，屆時新車銷售（尤其EV高價車款）與零組件供應鏈訂單將同步下滑，經銷商庫存天數攀升，融資違約率上升壓抑購車需求。
auto.en: Employment fell 31k since June 2025 (12,636k to 12,605k), with eight-month downtrend intact despite +7k monthly gain. Manufacturing workers are core buyers of pickup trucks and entry-level vehicles; shrinking headcount weakens auto loan applications and approval rates. Persistent negative YoY growth precedes broader recessions, threatening new vehicle sales—especially high-priced EVs—and supply chain orders. Dealer inventory days will rise as financing defaults increase, creating negative feedback loop. MCU and sensor content demand softens as OEMs cut production schedules in response to weakening blue-collar demand.

tsmc.zh: 製造業就業疲軟暗示美國商品經濟減速，影響TSMC客戶組合：消費性晶片（手機、PC）需求因藍領購買力下降而承壓，但AI伺服器與資料中心客戶（佔先進製程投片量40%+）較不受製造業就業影響。目前12605千人就業數據顯示3奈米與5奈米產能利用率將呈現分化：HPC/AI節點維持滿載，但N7以下成熟製程面臨消費性電子庫存調整壓力，整體資本支出展望謹慎。
tsmc.en: Weakening manufacturing employment (down 31k from 12,636k peak) signals divergent demand across TSMC's customer mix. Consumer chip orders (smartphone, PC) face pressure as blue-collar purchasing power erodes, stressing N7 and mature node utilization. However, AI server and datacenter clients—representing 40%+ of advanced node wafer starts—remain insulated from manufacturing labor trends. The 12,605k reading suggests 3nm/5nm HPC capacity stays tight while mature processes see inventory corrections. Mixed capex outlook: leading-edge expansion proceeds but overall wafer demand growth moderates from 15% to single-digit as consumer electronics weaken.

commentary.zh: 10年-2年利差回升至0.41%，較前期上升0.03個百分點。近期波動範圍介於0.38%-0.49%，整體保持溫和正值，距離衰退風險訊號的倒掛仍有距離。雖未顯示立即衰退壓力，但利差仍相對平緩，反映市場對聯準會政策路徑仍存不確定性。若利差持續走低或重返負值，將為經濟前景敲響警鐘。近期波動性暗示債市參與者對利率政策前景評估仍在調整中。
commentary.en: The spread recovered to 0.41%, up 0.03pp from the prior reading. Recent fluctuations between 0.38%-0.49% show manageable volatility, remaining safely above inversion territory. While this cushion suggests no imminent recession signal, the modest spread level reflects lingering uncertainty about Fed policy trajectory. A sustained decline toward or below zero would signal heightened recession risk—historically a 6-18 month lead indicator. Current levels indicate markets are still assessing longer-term rate expectations as inflation dynamics evolve.

ai.zh: 利差維持正值0.41%且近期穩定在0.38-0.49%區間，顯示殖利率曲線未倒掛，衰退風險暫未浮現。這支持超大規模業者（Microsoft、Google）持續執行數據中心資本支出計畫，NVIDIA GPU需求維持強勁。穩定的正利差環境有利長期AI基礎建設投資，融資成本預期不會因曲線反轉而突然惡化，H100/H200訂單能見度維持高檔。
ai.en: The spread holding at +0.41% and stabilizing in the 0.38-0.49% range signals no curve inversion, reducing near-term recession risk. This supports hyperscalers' continued AI capex deployment—Microsoft, Google, and others maintain aggressive data center builds. Stable positive slope keeps long-duration infrastructure financing attractive, sustaining strong NVIDIA GPU demand (H100/H200) and preventing sudden cost-of-capital spikes that would curtail multi-year AI investment roadmaps.

mobile.zh: 利差0.41%略升0.03個百分點，曲線形態正常但斜率溫和，反映經濟擴張力道平穩而非強勁。消費性電子需求不會因衰退預期而崩跌，但也缺乏加速增長動能。iPhone與Samsung旗艦機出貨預期持平，換機週期未見縮短。正利差環境對消費者信心中性偏穩，手機需求維持個位數溫和成長，無明顯上行或下行催化劑。
mobile.en: Spread at +0.41% (+0.03pp) reflects a normally-sloped but shallow curve, signaling stable—not robust—expansion. Consumer electronics demand avoids recession-driven collapse but lacks acceleration catalyst. Apple iPhone and Samsung flagship shipments expected flat, with upgrade cycles unchanged. Positive spread supports steady consumer confidence, pointing to low-single-digit smartphone growth without strong upside or downside triggers. Inventory levels and ASPs remain range-bound in this environment.

auto.zh: 利差正值0.41%消除短期衰退擔憂，有利汽車貸款市場穩定，但絕對利率水準仍偏高，5-7年期車貸成本壓抑傳統燃油車與入門級電動車需求。正斜率意味長天期融資較短期更貴，拉長購車決策週期。底特律與豐田受惠衰退風險降低，但Tesla、BYD等電動車銷售仍受融資成本制約，供應鏈MCU、電池訂單增速放緩至中個位數。
auto.en: Positive 0.41% spread alleviates imminent recession fears, stabilizing auto loan markets. However, absolute rate levels remain elevated, keeping 5-7 year auto financing costly and dampening demand for ICE vehicles and entry-level EVs. Upward-sloping curve makes longer-duration loans pricier, extending purchase decision timelines. Detroit and Toyota benefit from reduced downturn risk, but Tesla and BYD face headwinds from financing costs, slowing supply chain orders (MCUs, batteries) to mid-single-digit growth.

tsmc.zh: 利差0.41%穩定於正值區間，降低客戶因衰退預期削減晶片訂單的風險。AI相關3奈米與5奈米產能利用率維持滿載，NVIDIA、AMD、Broadcom投片量不受影響。正常殖利率曲線支持TSMC 2026年資本支出維持300-320億美元高檔，先進封裝CoWoS供不應求。手機與汽車客戶訂單雖平穩但不衰退，客戶組合中AI佔比持續提升至40%以上。
tsmc.en: Spread stable at +0.41% reduces risk of customer order cuts driven by recession expectations. AI-driven 3nm and 5nm utilization remains at full capacity, with NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom wafer starts unaffected. Normal yield curve underpins TSMC's $30-32bn capex guidance for 2026 and tight CoWoS advanced packaging supply. Mobile and auto customer orders stay flat but avoid downturn, while AI mix in revenue rises above 40%. Positive slope supports long-cycle CapEx confidence and customer pre-commitments.

commentary.zh: 10年-3月利差於6月8日報0.76%，較前日小幅下降0.01個百分點。近期數據顯示利差在0.69-0.89%區間波動，自5月21日高點0.89%下跌逾13基點。雖未觸及倒掛，但持續走低反映市場對經濟前景的謹慎態度。聯儲偏好之此指標過往完美預測8次衰退，目前水準雖仍為正，但若持續下行至倒掛，將成為重要預警信號。投資人應密切關注其趨勢，評估未來利率政策及經濟軟著陸風險。
commentary.en: The 10Y-3M spread stood at 0.76% on June 8, down 1 basis point from prior session. The indicator has declined from May 21's peak of 0.89%, currently trading in the 0.69-0.89% range. Though remaining positive, the downward trend signals growing market caution about economic prospects. Given this Fed-preferred gauge's perfect track record predicting all 8 prior US recessions when inverted, sustained deterioration toward negative territory would constitute a critical warning signal. Market participants should monitor closely for potential policy implications and recession risk reassessment.

ai.zh: 10年-3月利差維持在正值0.76%，顯示經濟衰退風險低，有利AI基建投資持續。雖然較前期小幅下降0.01個百分點，但近期在0.69-0.89%區間波動保持正斜率，表明長期成長預期穩固。超大規模雲端業者(Microsoft、Google)的資本支出意願不受抑制，NVIDIA H100/H200需求強勁，數據中心擴建計畫可持續推進，融資成本壓力未顯著上升。
ai.en: The 10Y-3M spread at 0.76% remains positive, signaling low recession risk and supporting sustained AI infrastructure investment. Despite a minor -0.01pp decline, the recent 0.69-0.89% range maintains a positive slope, affirming long-term growth expectations. Hyperscaler capex from Microsoft and Google remains robust, driving strong NVIDIA H100/H200 GPU demand. Data center buildouts continue unimpeded, with financing costs stable and no recession-driven budget cuts threatening AI spending momentum.

mobile.zh: 利差0.76%反映經濟穩健但成長動能溫和，對手機需求影響中性。近兩週從0.89%降至0.76%顯示長期利率預期趨緩，消費者信心未明顯改善，iPhone及Samsung旗艦機換機週期可能延長。雖無衰退威脅(利差仍為正)，但0.77%→0.76%的微降顯示消費力道平淡，中低階機種競爭加劇，高階市場ASP成長有限，整體出貨量維持個位數成長。
mobile.en: The 0.76% spread reflects stable but tepid growth, yielding neutral smartphone demand. The two-week decline from 0.89% to 0.76% signals cooling long-term rate expectations without spurring consumer confidence—iPhone and Samsung flagship replacement cycles may extend. Absence of inversion rules out recession fears, but the 0.77%→0.76% dip suggests muted spending power. Mid-range competition intensifies while premium ASP growth stalls, leaving shipment growth in low single digits for Apple and Samsung through Q3 2026.

auto.zh: 利差0.76%為正值，排除近期衰退風險，但從0.8%降至0.76%反映長期利率下行壓力，汽車貸款成本略有改善空間，理論上有利購車需求。然而幅度有限(-0.01pp)且利差仍處近期區間低端(0.69-0.89%)，消費者融資條件改善不明顯。電動車市場競爭白熱化，傳統車廠庫存壓力持續，供應鏈MCU需求疲軟，整體銷量成長受制於利率絕對水準仍高。
auto.en: The positive 0.76% spread eliminates near-term recession risk, but the decline from 0.8% suggests modest downward rate pressure—marginally improving auto loan affordability. Yet the -0.01pp move is minor, and the spread sits near the recent low (0.69-0.89%), offering limited financing relief. EV competition intensifies while legacy OEMs face inventory overhang, weakening MCU semiconductor content demand. Auto sales growth remains constrained by still-elevated absolute interest rates, leaving pickup and SUV segments soft despite no imminent downturn.

tsmc.zh: 利差0.76%維持正值，經濟穩定支撐晶圓需求，特別是AI驅動的3nm/5nm先進製程滿載。雖然利差從0.8%小降至0.76%，但近期0.69-0.77%波動顯示客戶(NVIDIA、AMD、Apple)資本支出計畫未受衰退疑慮影響。CoWoS封裝產能供不應求，HPC客戶組合佔比提升，抵銷手機晶片需求疲軟。資本支出維持280-320億美元區間，先進製程利用率達95%以上。
tsmc.en: The 0.76% positive spread sustains economic stability, supporting wafer demand—especially AI-driven 3nm/5nm nodes running at full utilization. Though down from 0.8%, the recent 0.69-0.77% band shows customers (NVIDIA, AMD, Apple) maintain capex undeterred by recession fears. CoWoS advanced packaging remains supply-constrained, with HPC mix rising to offset smartphone chip weakness. TSMC's $28-32bn capex guidance holds firm, and leading-edge fab utilization exceeds 95%, driven by data center processors and AI accelerators through 2026.

commentary.zh: 10年期美債殖利率自6月5日升至4.55%，較前一交易日上漲8個基點。儘管5月下旬一度跌至4.45%，近日反彈顯示市場對通膨前景的擔憂升溫。殖利率上升將直接推高房貸及企業融資成本，對長存續期科技股估值造成壓力。這反映市場對聯準會降息時程的預期調整，未來若持續上升恐加劇股債聯動賣壓。投資者應密切關注通膨數據及聯準會政策信號。
commentary.en: The 10-year yield rose to 4.55% on June 5th, up 8 basis points, reversing May's dip to 4.45%. This rebound signals renewed inflation concerns and suggests markets are reassessing Fed rate-cut expectations. Higher yields will increase mortgage and corporate borrowing costs, pressuring long-duration tech valuations. The 1.8% move underscores market sensitivity to monetary policy expectations. Investors should monitor inflation data closely; sustained yield increases could trigger broader equity selloffs, particularly in growth-sensitive sectors.

ai.zh: 10年期殖利率驟升至4.55%（單日+8bp），將壓縮高估值AI股票本益比，特別是微軟、Google等超大規模業者的遠期現金流折現價值。更高的借貸成本（企業債利差隨之擴大）可能延緩部分資料中心擴建計畫，雖NVIDIA、Broadcom等核心AI晶片需求短期剛性，但客戶資本支出審查趨嚴，Q3-Q4訂單能見度轉保守，GPU交期壓力可能緩解。
ai.en: The 10-year yield spiking to 4.55% (+8bp) compresses valuations for high-multiple AI stocks like Microsoft and Google, whose long-duration cash flows face steeper discounting. Higher corporate borrowing costs may delay marginal data center builds, though near-term GPU demand from NVIDIA/Broadcom remains sticky. Hyperscalers will scrutinize capex more rigorously, potentially softening Q3-Q4 orders and easing GPU lead times as financing headwinds mount.

mobile.zh: 殖利率反彈至4.55%推升消費信貸成本，削弱分期付款購機意願，尤其衝擊高單價旗艦機（iPhone 16 Pro、Galaxy S系列）。美國電信商補貼預算受融資成本上升擠壓，促銷力度減弱，Q2-Q3換機週期拉長。蘋果、三星在北美出貨將承壓，供應鏈庫存調整延續，聯發科、高通手機晶片出貨增速放緩，零組件砍單風險增加。
mobile.en: Yield rebounding to 4.55% raises consumer credit costs, dampening installment-financed purchases of high-end smartphones like iPhone 16 Pro and Galaxy S flagships. U.S. carrier subsidies face tighter budgets as financing expenses climb, weakening promotions and lengthening replacement cycles in Q2-Q3. Apple and Samsung North American shipments soften, extending supply chain inventory corrections. MediaTek and Qualcomm mobile chip shipments decelerate, raising component order-cut risks.

auto.zh: 殖利率升至4.55%直接推高汽車貸款利率至8%以上，重創購車負擔能力，預期6-7月美國輕型車銷售年率降至1,520萬輛以下。電動車受雙重打擊：高車價疊加高利率，Tesla Model 3/Y與福特Mach-E月供增加$120-150，需求轉疲。經銷商庫存天數攀升至75天，供應鏈MCU、功率半導體訂單遞延，博世、大陸集團零件營收Q3將下修。
auto.en: Yield rising to 4.55% pushes auto loan rates above 8%, crushing affordability and likely pulling June-July U.S. light vehicle SAAR below 15.2 million. EVs face dual headwinds: premium pricing plus higher rates add $120-150 to monthly payments for Tesla Model 3/Y and Ford Mach-E, softening demand. Dealer inventory climbs to 75 days, delaying supply chain orders for MCUs and power semiconductors. Bosch and Continental component revenues face Q3 downward revisions.

tsmc.zh: 殖利率升至4.55%對台積電影響分化：AI伺服器晶片（N3E/N4P）需求維持滿載，但智慧手機與車用客戶因終端需求疲軟調降N5/N7投片量15-20%，整體產能利用率Q3預估降至88%。客戶組合轉向高毛利AI晶片（毛利率>60%）部分抵銷消費端疲弱，但2027年先進製程資本支出$320-350億美元計畫面臨融資成本壓力，擴產節奏可能微調。
tsmc.en: Yield at 4.55% creates mixed effects for TSMC: AI server chip demand (N3E/N4P) stays at full capacity, but smartphone and auto clients cut N5/N7 wafer starts 15-20% amid weak end demand, pulling Q3 utilization to ~88%. Customer mix shifting toward high-margin AI chips (60%+ gross margin) partially offsets consumer weakness, yet the $32-35 billion advanced-node capex plan for 2027 faces higher financing costs, potentially moderating expansion cadence.

commentary.zh: 恐慌指數於6月5日驟升至21.51，較前日大漲39.7%，突破警戒水位。此前一週VIX維持低迷（15-16區間），反映市場自滿，本次急速上升暗示投資人風險偏好明顯下降。升幅之大預示近期可能出現重大利空事件或經濟數據惡化，可能引發股市調整及避險資金流入債券與黃金。聯準會與市場參與者應關注後續經濟數據與企業獲利表現，以評估市場波動是否反映基本面風險或僅為短期技術性修正。
commentary.en: The VIX surged to 21.51 on June 5th, jumping 39.7% and entering the caution zone after lingering in complacent territory (15-16 range) throughout late May. This sharp spike signals a significant shift in risk appetite, potentially triggered by adverse economic data or market events. The magnitude of the move suggests investors are reassessing tail risks. Market participants and policymakers should monitor subsequent data releases and earnings to determine whether volatility reflects fundamental deterioration or represents a technical correction amid underlying economic resilience.

ai.zh: VIX從15.4驟升至21.51（+39.7%）進入警戒區間，反映市場風險偏好下降。短期內雲端大廠可能延後部分GPU採購決策，NVIDIA等AI股面臨估值回調壓力。然而AI基礎設施具長期結構性需求，hyperscaler資本支出計畫較不受短期波動影響，Microsoft與Google的資料中心擴建多為多年期承諾，實際衝擊有限。若VIX持續攀升突破30，可能觸發機構投資人減碼高Beta科技股。
ai.en: VIX surge from 15.4 to 21.51 (+39.7%) entering caution zone signals declining risk appetite. Near-term GPU procurement decisions by hyperscalers may face scrutiny, pressuring NVIDIA valuations. However, AI infrastructure enjoys structural demand—Microsoft and Google data center buildouts reflect multi-year commitments less sensitive to short-term volatility. H100/H200 order backlogs remain robust. Sustained VIX above 30 could trigger institutional de-risking of high-beta AI stocks, but capex deferrals unlikely unless prolonged market stress emerges.

mobile.zh: VIX單日暴增39.7%至21.51顯示消費者信心可能轉弱，discretionary spending首當其衝。智慧型手機屬非必需消費，iPhone與Galaxy旗艦機換機週期易延長。市場動盪期間，消費者傾向保留現金而非升級設備，特別影響$1000以上高階機種需求。Apple供應鏈可能下修6-7月出貨預測，零組件庫存調整壓力浮現。若VIX維持20以上達數週，Q3傳統旺季備貨力道將明顯趨緩。
mobile.en: VIX spike to 21.51 (+39.7%) threatens discretionary consumer spending where smartphones sit. Premium iPhone and Galaxy flagship upgrades ($1000+) most vulnerable as households defer non-essential purchases during market uncertainty. Apple may revise June-July shipment forecasts downward, pressuring component inventory. Historical correlation shows VIX sustained above 20 delays replacement cycles by 1-2 quarters. Samsung's mid-tier Android segment shows more resilience, but overall smartphone TAM contracts 3-5% when volatility persists beyond 4 weeks in caution zone.

auto.zh: VIX從15.4跳升至21.51反映金融狀況收緊預期，直接衝擊汽車貸款成本與消費者購車意願。波動率上升通常伴隨信用利差擴大，使車貸利率走高，月付款負擔加重抑制新車銷售。EV市場更敏感—Tesla等高單價電動車需仰賴融資，借貸成本上升直接削弱需求。經銷商庫存天數可能從55天攀升至70天以上。傳統車廠如GM、Ford的次級供應商面臨訂單下修，晶片與電池採購計畫將更保守。
auto.en: VIX jump to 21.51 tightens financial conditions, raising auto loan rates and dampening purchase intent. Rising volatility widens credit spreads—average new vehicle APRs likely climb 50-80bps, adding $40-60 to monthly payments and suppressing demand. EV segment most exposed: Tesla and premium EVs depend on financing; higher borrowing costs directly erode affordability. Dealer inventory may swell from 55 to 70+ days. GM and Ford's Tier-2 suppliers face order cuts; semiconductor and battery procurement turns conservative as OEMs hedge against demand uncertainty through Q3.

tsmc.zh: VIX升至21.51對台積電呈現雙面影響。負面端：客戶如NVIDIA、AMD面臨股價壓力可能微調3nm/5nm投片量，蘋果若下修iPhone預測將影響N3E產能利用率；市場波動使hyperscaler資本支出審查趨嚴。正面端：台積電先進製程寡占地位使定價權穩固，CoWoS封裝需求積壓至2027年，短期波動難撼動長約。然而若VIX持續處於20以上，客戶可能延後2027年2nm產能預付款，影響資本支出回收時程。當前21.51屬警戒而非恐慌，fundamentals尚穩健。
tsmc.en: VIX at 21.51 creates mixed impact for TSMC. Downside: NVIDIA and AMD may trim 3nm/5nm wafer starts amid valuation pressure; Apple iPhone forecast cuts threaten N3E utilization in Q3. Hyperscaler capex scrutiny intensifies under volatility. Upside: TSMC's advanced node monopoly preserves pricing power; CoWoS capacity sold through 2027, insulating from short-term swings. Long-term agreements with major customers buffer immediate impact. Key risk: sustained VIX above 20 could delay 2nm capacity prepayments due 2H26, affecting capex ROI timeline. At 21.51, still caution zone—not panic—leaving structural demand story intact.

commentary.zh: 高收益債利差升至276基點，較前日上升2基點，近兩週在270-276基點區間窄幅波動。儘管最新讀數略有上升，但仍遠低於400基點風險偏好閾值，反映市場信用情緒保持穩健。近期波動幅度極小（僅5基點區間），表明投資人對高收益債風險溢酬預期相對穩定，尚無明顯信用緊張跡象。此水位暗示市場對經濟基本面和企業償債能力信心依舊，但仍需密切關注是否突破300基點關鍵位置。
commentary.en: High yield spreads widened slightly to 276bps, up 2bps from prior day, oscillating narrowly between 270-276bps over two weeks. The reading remains well below the 400bps risk-on threshold, signaling resilient credit sentiment. Minimal volatility (5bps range) indicates stable expectations for credit risk premiums with no acute stress signals. This level suggests confidence in economic fundamentals and corporate debt service capacity, though vigilance is warranted if spreads breach the 300bps level.

ai.zh: 高收益債利差維持在2.76%的極低水準，遠低於400bps風險偏好門檻，顯示信用市場極度寬鬆。這為超大規模雲端業者（微軟、Google）提供低成本融資環境，支撐其數據中心資本支出擴張計畫。NVIDIA等AI基礎設施供應商受益於客戶融資能力充裕，H100/H200訂單能見度維持強勁。寬鬆信用條件亦降低AI新創企業融資門檻，推動算力需求持續增長。
ai.en: At 2.76%, the spread remains drastically below the 400bps risk-on threshold, signaling exceptionally loose credit conditions. This enables hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, AWS) to finance massive datacenter buildouts at minimal cost, sustaining robust GPU procurement from NVIDIA. The benign credit environment supports continued AI capex expansion, with corporate bond issuance for infrastructure projects facing negligible risk premiums. AI startups also access cheap capital, amplifying compute demand across the ecosystem.

mobile.zh: 利差從2.71%微升至2.76%（+0.02個百分點），但仍處歷史極低區間，反映消費信貸環境穩定。對蘋果、三星而言，低利差意味消費者分期付款成本維持低檔，支撐高階旗艦機型（iPhone Pro系列、Galaxy S系列）銷售。然而手機為成熟市場，需求主要受產品創新週期驅動而非融資成本，此微幅利差波動對出貨量影響有限，換機週期延長趨勢未因信用條件改變。
mobile.en: The marginal +0.02pp rise to 2.76% keeps spreads at historically tight levels, maintaining stable consumer credit access. Low financing costs support installment plans for premium devices (iPhone Pro, Galaxy S24 Ultra), but smartphone demand is largely innovation-driven rather than credit-sensitive. Apple and Samsung shipments remain more dependent on AI feature differentiation and 5G refresh cycles than on this minimal spread movement. The stable-but-low spread is neutral for a mature replacement-driven market.

auto.zh: 利差2.76%接近歷史低點，企業債融資成本極具吸引力，有利車廠（特斯拉、福特、通用）進行電動車產能擴張與電池廠投資。消費端而言，低信用利差傳導至車貸利率下行，降低購車總成本，提振SUV與電動車銷售。供應鏈廠商（博世、大陸集團）亦受惠於低成本營運資金融資，支撐ADAS、電池管理系統等高價值零組件庫存投資，強化供應鏈韌性。
auto.en: At 2.76%, ultra-tight spreads reduce auto loan rates for consumers, lowering total ownership costs and supporting pickup/SUV and EV demand. For OEMs (Tesla, Ford, GM), cheap corporate debt financing accelerates EV capacity expansion and battery gigafactory investments. Tier-1 suppliers (Bosch, Continental) access low-cost working capital to fund inventory for ADAS and BMS components. The benign credit environment strengthens both demand-side financing and supply-side capex, creating a tailwind for auto sales and EV adoption momentum.

tsmc.zh: 高收益債利差2.76%顯示企業融資環境極度友善，直接利好台積電客戶（NVIDIA、AMD、蘋果、博通）的晶片採購預算與長期合約承諾。超大規模雲端業者在低成本資金支持下加速AI晶片下單，推升N3/N2先進製程產能利用率至滿載。台積電自身亦可透過美元公司債以極低利差進行亞利桑那州與日本廠資本支出融資，2奈米量產投資計畫風險溢酬壓力減輕，客戶組合持續向高毛利AI/HPC應用傾斜。
tsmc.en: The 2.76% spread ensures TSMC's hyperscaler customers (NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom) maintain aggressive chip procurement budgets funded by cheap corporate debt, driving N3/N2 advanced node utilization near 100%. Apple's favorable financing costs support stable iPhone processor orders. TSMC itself benefits from minimal risk premiums on USD bond issuance to fund Arizona and Japan fab expansions, reducing capex financing pressure. The exceptionally tight credit conditions reinforce customer mix shift toward high-margin AI/HPC applications, sustaining strong wafer demand and pricing power.

commentary.zh: 職位空缺在4月份驟增至7,618千個，環比上升10.6%，扭轉了過去五個月的下行趨勢。儘管如此，與2025年中期的7,000-7,300千個水平相比，目前仍處於相對溫和狀態。此次反彈可能反映季節性招聘需求或企業信心短期改善。若職位空缺持續上升，將加重聯準會降息的風險，且可能推升工資通膨預期。聯準會將密切監測職位空缺對失業比率，以評估勞動市場是否重新過熱。
commentary.en: Job openings surged to 7,618 thousand in April, up 10.6% from March, reversing five months of decline. However, levels remain moderate compared to mid-2025 readings of 7,000-7,300 thousand. This rebound likely reflects seasonal hiring or improved business sentiment. Sustained increases could complicate Fed rate-cut plans and reignite wage inflation concerns. The Fed will monitor the openings-to-unemployment ratio closely to assess whether labor market momentum is reaccelerating.

ai.zh: 職位空缺從6.55百萬（12月低點）反彈至7.62百萬，跳升10.6%顯示勞動市場重新緊俏。AI數據中心建設與GPU部署需求大量技術人力（雲端工程師、AI研究員），職缺回升反映hyperscalers（Microsoft、Google）持續擴張基礎設施投資。但若職缺/失業比重新升破1.2，工資通膨壓力將推遲Fed降息，提高AI capex融資成本，NVIDIA H100/B200訂單能見度雖佳但財務槓桿成本上揚。
ai.en: Job openings surged 10.6% to 7.62 million from December's 6.55 million trough, signaling labor market re-tightening. This validates robust AI infrastructure hiring—cloud engineers, ML specialists—as hyperscalers (Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud) accelerate data center buildouts and GPU deployments. However, rising openings risk rekindling wage inflation, potentially delaying Fed rate cuts and raising capex financing costs for NVIDIA's customers. Near-term AI spending momentum remains strong, but tighter labor may compress margins for system integrators and extend datacenter construction timelines.

mobile.zh: 職缺從6月7.20百萬下探至12月6.55百萬後反彈至7.62百萬，波動顯示勞動市場不穩定。職缺激增若推升工資通膨，消費者可支配收入將受擠壓，延後iPhone 16與Galaxy S系列換機週期。歷史顯示職缺過熱（>8百萬）後Fed緊縮曾壓抑非必需消費，目前7.62百萬雖未觸發警戒但上升趨勢不利。Apple與Samsung在北美市場ASP依賴中高階機型，工資成本上揚侵蝕消費意願，出貨量恐持平。
mobile.en: Openings rebounded sharply to 7.62 million (+10.6%), reversing the December low of 6.55 million and signaling potential wage pressure resurgence. Higher labor costs squeeze discretionary consumer budgets, delaying premium smartphone upgrade cycles—Apple's iPhone 16 Pro and Samsung's Galaxy S25 face headwinds as consumers prioritize essentials. Historically, openings above 8 million preceded Fed tightening that dampened electronics demand. While 7.62 million remains below prior peaks, the upward trajectory threatens North American ASP and shipment volumes for both vendors in H2 2026.

auto.zh: 職缺彈升至7.62百萬反映就業需求復甦，理論上支撐汽車購買力，但同步風險是工資通膨迫使Fed維持高利率。當前車貸利率仍在7-8%區間，若職缺持續攀升推遲降息預期，高利率環境將壓抑pickup與SUV融資需求，傳統燃油車銷售承壓。反之，科技業職缺增加（數據中心、AI）有利高收入族群購買Tesla Model Y與電動車，EV滲透率可能因富裕階層需求微升，但整體SAAR（年化銷售）受利率鉗制難突破1,580萬輛。
auto.en: The 7.62 million opening surge (+10.6%) presents a dual edge: stronger employment supports purchasing power, yet rising labor costs may keep Fed rates elevated longer, sustaining 7-8% auto loan APRs. High financing costs suppress pickup and SUV demand—core margin drivers for Detroit OEMs. Conversely, tech-sector job growth (AI, datacenter roles) benefits high-income EV buyers, modestly aiding Tesla and Rivian. However, inventory normalization and rate sensitivity constrain overall SAAR below 15.8 million units. Supply chain hiring improvements support production, but demand headwinds from debt service costs dominate the outlook.

tsmc.zh: 職缺達7.62百萬顯示科技業人才爭奪白熱化，對TSMC客戶（NVIDIA、AMD、Apple）有利有弊。AI晶片需求強勁推動N3E與CoWoS先進封裝產能滿載，但職缺激增若引發工資螺旋，客戶capex面臨融資成本上揚壓力，可能遞延部分非關鍵訂單（如N5成熟製程IoT晶片）。手機端Apple A18與高通驍龍需求因消費疲軟持平。整體而言3奈米利用率維持高檔（>90%），但5/7奈米產能利用率可能從85%回落至80%，客戶組合分化加劇。
tsmc.en: 7.62 million openings (+10.6%) intensify tech talent competition, creating mixed effects for TSMC. Robust AI chip demand (NVIDIA H200, AMD MI300) keeps N3E and CoWoS advanced packaging near full utilization (>90%), but rising labor costs threaten customer capex if wage inflation delays Fed cuts and raises financing costs. Apple A18 and Qualcomm Snapdragon orders remain flat amid smartphone softness. N5/N7 utilization risks slipping from 85% to 80% as IoT and automotive clients defer non-critical tape-outs. Customer mix polarizes: hyperscaler AI orders surge while consumer-facing nodes face headwinds, complicating TSMC's 2026 capex allocation.

commentary.zh: 民間部門平均時薪至5月達37.53美元，月增0.3%，年增率約3.2%。雖低於4%警戒線，但自去年6月以來持續穩定上升，累計漲幅達3.2%。考量服務業通膨仍粘著於2%+水準，工資成長動能維持溫和但堅韌。聯準會將密切關注此指標，作為評估勞動市場緊俏度與通膨預期的重要參考。若工資年增率回升至4%以上，恐加強服務業通膨持續性，可能延緩降息進程。
commentary.en: Private sector average hourly earnings reached $37.53 in May, up 0.3% monthly and approximately 3.2% year-over-year—below the 4% threshold but trending steadily upward since June 2024. Cumulative gains of 3.2% over eleven months reflect resilient labor market momentum. Given sticky service sector inflation remaining above 2%, the Fed will monitor wage dynamics closely as a barometer of labor tightness and inflation expectations. Should year-over-year growth re-accelerate toward 4%, it could reinforce service inflation persistence and potentially delay rate cuts.

ai.zh: 時薪達37.53美元，年增3.2%，低於4%通膨警戒線，顯示勞動成本壓力溫和。對超大規模業者而言，資料中心建設與營運的人力成本上升0.3%屬可控範圍，但持續12個月的上漲趨勢（36.36→37.53美元）累積增幅已達3.2%，將壓縮雲端服務毛利。若工資成長保持在3-3.5%區間，聯準會降息預期支撐AI資本支出，利好NVIDIA GPU需求；但若突破4%將觸發緊縮政策，衝擊超大規模業者擴張計畫。
ai.en: Hourly earnings at $37.53 reflect 3.2% YoY growth, below the Fed's 4% inflation threshold, indicating controlled labor cost pressures. For hyperscalers, the 0.3% MoM increase in data center construction and operations wages remains manageable, but the cumulative 12-month rise from $36.36 compresses cloud service margins. Wage growth sustained at 3-3.5% supports Fed easing expectations, bolstering AI capex and NVIDIA GPU demand. However, a breach above 4% would trigger tighter policy, jeopardizing hyperscaler expansion timelines and GPU order visibility.

mobile.zh: 時薪年增3.2%且連續12個月攀升（36.36→37.53美元），實質薪資增幅有限，侵蝕消費者對高階手機的購買力。雖月增0.3%顯示勞動市場穩定，但工資成長未能顯著超越通膨，中低收入消費者更傾向延長換機週期或選擇中階機型。Apple與Samsung旗艦機種面臨需求疲軟，尤其1000美元以上機型銷量承壓。若工資成長持續低於4%，高階手機ASP成長動能將受限，迫使品牌廠商依賴促銷與分期付款刺激出貨。
mobile.en: Wage growth at 3.2% YoY with 12-month upward trend ($36.36→$37.53) delivers limited real income gains, eroding consumer purchasing power for premium smartphones. While 0.3% MoM suggests stable employment, wage increases barely outpace inflation, pushing mid-to-low income consumers toward longer replacement cycles and mid-tier models. Apple and Samsung flagship devices above $1,000 face demand headwinds. If wage growth stays below 4%, premium ASP momentum weakens, forcing brands to rely on promotional financing. Moderate wage gains insufficient to revive discretionary spending on $1,200+ devices.

auto.zh: 時薪達37.53美元、月增0.3%，顯示購車能力微幅改善，但年增3.2%的幅度不足以抵消過去兩年車貸利率累積升幅。以5年期新車貸款計，月薪增加約20美元（以每月173小時計）難以覆蓋7%車貸利率下每月增加的50-70美元還款負擔。皮卡與大型SUV銷售持續受壓，消費者轉向較低價車型。然而工資穩定成長支撐就業信心，有助入門級燃油車與平價電動車（3-4萬美元區間）銷量，但豪華車與高價EV（6萬美元以上）需求仍疲弱，拖累單車利潤與經銷商庫存周轉。
auto.en: Wages at $37.53, up 0.3% MoM, marginally improve affordability, but 3.2% YoY growth insufficient to offset cumulative auto loan rate increases. At 7% financing, the ~$20 monthly wage gain (173 hours/month) fails to cover $50-70 higher monthly payments on 5-year new car loans. Pickup and large SUV sales remain pressured as buyers shift to lower-priced segments. However, steady wage growth supports employment confidence, aiding entry-level ICE and affordable EV sales ($30-40k range). Luxury and premium EV demand above $60k stays soft, dragging per-unit margins and dealer inventory turns.

tsmc.zh: 時薪年增3.2%、維持在4%警戒線下，意味聯準會不會激進緊縮，維持美國客戶資本支出穩定性。超大規模業者（Microsoft、Google）AI晶片需求支撐3奈米與5奈米產能利用率，但手機與汽車晶片終端需求因消費者實質薪資增長有限而疲軟，影響7奈米以上成熟製程訂單。月增0.3%顯示勞動市場未過熱，降低客戶庫存調整風險。然而工資穩定上揚12個月累積壓力可能延遲消費性電子復甦，先進製程與成熟製程需求分化加劇，整體晶圓出貨量成長率受限於非AI應用疲弱。
tsmc.en: Wage growth at 3.2% YoY, below 4% threshold, signals Fed restraint, stabilizing US customer capex. Hyperscaler AI chip demand (Microsoft, Google) sustains 3nm and 5nm utilization, but limited real wage gains dampen smartphone and auto end-demand, pressuring 7nm+ mature node orders. The 0.3% MoM rise indicates labor market stability without overheating, reducing customer inventory correction risks. However, 12-month cumulative wage pressure may delay consumer electronics recovery, widening divergence between advanced and mature node demand. Overall wafer shipment growth constrained by weak non-AI applications despite strong AI momentum.
